messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the access-list copy command do?

The access-list copy command copies an IPv4, IPv6, or MAC ACL to a new destination ACL or overwrites an existing ACL. Specifies the type of ACL. Specifies the name of the ACL to be copied. <ACL-NAME> <DESTINATION-ACL> Specifies the name of the destination ACL. Syntax: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-ACL>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the access-list log-timer command do?

The access-list log-timer command sets the log timer interval for all ACEs that have the log parameter configured. Resets the log timer to its default 300 seconds. Specifies the log timer interval in seconds. Range: 5 to 300. Syntax: access-list log-timer {default|<INTERVAL>}.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the access-list secure-update command do?

The access-list secure-update command this command determines if access lists are updated using the secure-update feature. Secure-update is enabled by default. When secure update is enabled and an ACL is updated or replaced, one or more override entries are installed in the TCAM table(s) containing the ACL that is being modified. As a result, all traffic of the same type as the currently configured ACL will be denied on the interfaces to which the ACL is applied. This ensures that traffic is not temporarily allowed while modifying an ACL. Upon completion of the update, the TCAM override entries are uninstalled and traffic resumes ACL matching. The no version of this command disables this feature. If secure-update is disabled, there will be no override entry installed. Syntax: access-list secure-update.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command do?

The apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command applies an ACL to the interface (Individual front plane port) or Link Aggregation Group (LAG) identified by the current interface or LAG context. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the current interface or LAG identified by the current interface or LAG context. Specifies the ACL type: ip for IPv4, IPv6 for IPv6, or mac for MAC ACL. Specifies the ACL name. Selects the inbound (ingress) traffic direction. Selects the outbound (egress) traffic direction. NOTE For HPE Aruba Networking 6000 and 6100 switch series, the outbound (egress) traffic directio n is supported only for MAC ACLs. • Each ACL of a given type can be applied to the same interface or LAG once in each direction. Syntax: apply access-list {ip | IPv6 | mac} <ACL-NAME> {in|out}.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the policy command do?

The policy command creates or modifies classifier policy and policy entries. A policy is made up of one or more policy entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each entry has an IPv4/IPv6/MAC class and zero or more policy actions associated with it. A policy must be applied using the apply command. The no form of the command can be used to delete either a policy (use no with the policy command) or an individual policy entry (use no with the sequence number). Specifies the name of the policy. <POLICY-NAME> <SEQUENCE-NUMBER> Specifies a sequence number for the policy entry. Optional. Ran ge: 1 to 4294967295. comment Stores the remaining entered text as a policy entry comment. class {ip|IPv6|mac} <CLASS- NAME> Specifies a type of class, ip for IPv4, IPv6 for IPv6 and mac for a MAC policy. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about ACL and Policy hardware resource commands?

ACL and Policy hardware resource commands: Show Resources The show resources command allows users to see hardware resource consumption in the switch. These hardware resources include the following: • TCAM entries • L4 Port ranges • High-Capacity TCAM entries On the 4100i, 6000, 6100, 8320, 8325, and 8360 switch series, these resources are reported for the entire switch. On the 9300 switch series, resources are reported per pipe. The 9300 switch series has four pipes and the 9300s and 10040 switch series has two pipes. Public.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about Ingress IP Port Policy Lookup?

Ingress IP Port Policy Lookup: Ingress TCAM Entries 2 * Total Public For the 6000, 6100, and 4100i switch series: 184 Ingress TCAM Entries 180 256 3810 Egress TCAM Entries 0 0 2040 Ingress Lookups 4 28 Egress Lookups 0 8 Ingress Policers 21 2027 Egress Policers 0 1024 * This features shares reserved resources with the preceding feature. Subtopics Egress TCAM Egress TCAM The Egress TCAM operates similarly to the uniquely structured Ingress TCAM on the 4100i, 6000, and 6100 platforms. There is one pool of TCAM entries which any feature may consume. The following features are configurable on egress: • Egress Port MAC ACL.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about IP VLAN ACL Logging?

IP VLAN ACL Logging: L4 port ranges On platforms that support the use of dedicated resources called L4 Port Ranges, any ACE that uses lt, gt, range, or port groups attempts to use these dedicated hardware resources. L4 port ranges are used to reduce the number of TCAM entries needed to cover an L4 port range. For example, range 50000 5000 5 could be covered by a single L4 port range or by two TCAM entries using the following value/masks: Entry number L4 port range Value/Mask 1 2 range 50000 50003 0xC350/0xFFFC range 50004 50005 0xC354/0xFFFE The problem gets multiplied when an ACE matches on two L4 port ranges. The TCAM entries used to cover a source and destination L4 port range must cover all possibilities. For example, for this ACE, 10 permit tcp any range 100 200 any range 50000 50005 The range 100 200 needs six value masks. As a result, this ACE needs 12 (6*2=12) TCAM entries.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip proxy-arp command do?

The ip proxy-arp command enables proxy ARP for the specified Layer 3 interface. Proxy ARP is supported on Layer 3 physical interfaces, LAG interfaces, and VLAN interfaces. It is disabled by default. To enable proxy ARP on an interface, routing must be enabled on that interface. The no form of this command disables proxy ARP for the specified interface. Syntax: no ip proxy-arp.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the diag cable-diagnostic command do?

The diag cable-diagnostic command provides information about the cable health after running a diagnostic test on an interface. If you run a new cable diagnostic command when a cable diagnostic is in progress for the interface, the new cable diagnostic command fails to execute. In such a scenario, an error message is displayed. On executing a cable diagnostic test command, it automatically clears the old test results before the new test starts. Public Cable diagnostic commands 207 Specifies the name of the interface. <IF-NAME> test <IF-NAME> Runs a cable diagnostic test on an interface. show <IF-NAME> clear <IF-NAME>. Syntax: diag cable-diagnostic.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the class mac command do?

The class mac command creates or modifies a MAC traffic class to match specified packets. Class is composed of one or more class entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. With this command, each class can classify traffic based on MAC header information. The no form of the command can be used to delete either a MAC traffic class (use no with the class command) or an individual MAC traffic class entry (use no with the sequence number). Specifies create or modify a MAC class. Specifies the name of this class. Specifies a sequence number for the class entry. Optional. Rang e: 1-4294967295. {match|ignore} Creates a rule to match or ignore specified packets. comment {any} {any} Protocol Stores the remaining entered text as a class comment. Specifies any source MAC. Specifies any destination MAC. Syntax: class mac <CLASS-NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR> command configures a gateway address for the DHCP relay agent to use for DHCP requests. By default DHCP relay agent picks the lowest-numbered IP address on the interface. The no form of this command removes the gateway address. Specifies the IP address of the gateway in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a is a decimal number from 0 to 255. <IPv4-ADDR>. Syntax: no ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command do?

The ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name. Specifies the domain name to append to DNS requests. Length: 1 to 256 characters. Public ip dns domain-name 545 vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: no ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dns host <HOST-NAME> command do?

The ip dns host <HOST-NAME> command associates a static IP address with a hostname. The DNS client returns this IP address instead of querying a DNS server for an IP address for the hostname. Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. host <HOST-NAME> Specifies the name of a host. Length: 1 to 256 characters. <IP-ADDR> Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255, or IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexa decimal number from 0 to F. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax:
- ip dns host 546
- no ip dns host <HOST-NAME>.

syntax:
- ip dns host 546
- no ip dns host <HOST-NAME>

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> This command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <PORT0> Required, set port0 of the ring. Public erps ring <RINGID> <port0|port1> interface 564 Required, set port1 of the ring. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.

source_file: 4100i/10_18/Command-line Interface Reference Guide For 4100 Switch Series.md

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> hold-off-interval command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> hold-off-interval command specifies hold-off interval in units of 100 ms. If specified, a defect is not reported immediately. Instead, the hold-off timer is started. On expiration of the timer, if the defect still exists, it is reported to protection switching. The default value for hold-off timer is 0. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the hold-off interval and sets it to the default value of 0. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 Required, specifies the hold-off interval in units of 100 ms. De fault: 0. <RINGID> <100 milliseconds> On a common ERPS network, a physical ring can be configured with a single ERPS ring, and only one blocked port can be specified in the ring. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> control-vlan command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> control-vlan command this command adds a control-channel VLAN to a ring instance. In an ERPS ring, the control VLANshould be used only to forward RAPS PDUs and not service packets. All the devices in an ERPS ring instance must be configured with the same control VLAN, and different ERPS ring instances must use different control VLANs. The no form of this command removes the control-channel VLAN of the ring instance. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <ID> <VID> This command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring instance. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. The no form of this command removes the ring instance description. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> revertive command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> revertive command configures the default revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In revertive operation, after the conditions causing protection switching are cleared, traffic channels are restored to the recovered link blocking the RPL. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. The no form of this command configures non-revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In non-revertive operation, the traffic channels continue to use the RPL, if it has not failed, after conditions causing protection switching are cleared. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <ID> In ERPS, there is a central node called RPL Owner Node which blocks one of the ports to ensure that there is no loop formed for the Ethernet traffic. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> meg-level command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> meg-level command the R-APS messages transmitted by ERPS take the form of OAM PDUs as defined in G.8013. Each OAM PDU is transmitted at a specified level known as the Maintenance Entity Group (MEG) level. This command configures the level with which the ERPS packets must be transmitted. The no form of this command removes the configured MEG level and sets it to the default value of 7. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 Required, specifies the meg-level. Range: 0-7. Default: 7. <RINGID> <0-7> This command associates a sub-ring to a parent-ring and is required for the sub-ring to notify the parent- ring on change in topology. The no form of this command removes the parent ring identifier. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the copy primary secondary command do?

The copy primary secondary command copies the firmware image from the primary to the secondary location. Downloads a hot-patch or firmware image from a TFTP or SFTP server. Public copy <REMOTE-URL> 624 <REMOTE-URL> TFTP format: Specifies the URL from which to download the firmware using S FTP or TFTP. tftp://<IP-ADDR>[: <PORT-NUM>] [; blocksize=<Value>]/<FILENAME> SFTP format: sftp://<USERNAME>@<IP-ADDR> [: <PORT-NUM>]/<FILENAME> SCP format: scp://USER@{IP|HOST}[: PORT]/FILE {hot-patch|primary| secondary} Select a hot-patch or a primary or secondary image profile for receiving the downloaded firmware. Required. NOTE For more information about hot-patch, see hot -patch. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Syntax: copy <REMOTE-URL> {hot-patch|primary|secondary} [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

source_file: 4100i/10_18/Command-line Interface Reference Guide For 4100 Switch Series.md

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the https-server rest firmware-site-distribution command do?

The https-server rest firmware-site-distribution command this command enables or disables the Firmware Site Distribution server listening on port 9443. The firmware site distribution allows you to use a switch to distribute a firmware image file to other switches in the same network. This prevents the switches from connecting to the cloud or an external network to download a firmware image file. On enabling the firmware site distribution, it exposes a REST endpoint that allows the switches to download a switch primary or secondary firmware image. 1 NOTE As per the limitation, up to two switches can download the firmware image simultaneously. This endpoint is to be used along with REST /firmware endpoint to handle the firmware download and installation process. The no form of this command disables the firmware site distribution server. Syntax: no https-server rest firmware-site-distribution.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip icmp unreachable command do?

The ip icmp unreachable command enables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 destination unreachable messages on the switch to a source host when a specific host is unreachable. The unreachable host address originates from the failed packed. Default setting. The no form of this command disables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 destination unreachable messages from the switch to a source host when a specific host is unreachable. This command does not prevent other hosts from sending an ICMP unreachable message. Syntax: no ip icmp unreachable.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show ip igmp statistics command do?

The show ip igmp statistics command shows IGMP statistics, including groups joined, or shows statistics by VRF. Specify all-vrfs to show information for all VRFs. all-vrfs Configures drop-unknown and fastlearn modes on the ports. While IGMP snooping is enabled, the traffic will be forwarded only to ports that made an IGMP request for the multicast. Drop unknown filters ensure that packets are not forwarded to ports that did not make a request for the traffic stream. This could either be a filter across all VLANs (vlan-shared) or per VLAN (vlan-exclusive). The default is vlan-shared. Fast learn enables the port to learn group information when receiving a topology change notification. By default, fast learn is not enabled on ports. Syntax: show ip igmp statistics [all-vrfs].

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp snooping all-vlans command do?

The ip igmp snooping all-vlans command enable IGMP snooping on all VLANs. This configuration setting enables IGMP snooping on all existing VLANs, as well as on any VLANs that are created after the ip igmp snooping all-vlans command is issued. When IGMP snooping is enabled globally, IGMP snooping can not be configured on individual VLANs within the VLAN context of the command-line interface. The no form of this command disables global IGMP snooping, allows IGMP snooping to remain enabled at the VLAN level, and allows the configuration of VLAN-level IGMP snooping settings. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping all-vlans.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp snooping vxlan replicate-all command do?

The ip igmp snooping vxlan replicate-all command this command enables AOS-CX leaf devices (access-layer switches that connect directly to servers, storage, and other endpoints) to unconditionally forward multicast traffic to all VTEPs that do not support the exchange of IGMP reports over the fabric. By default, when IGMP snooping is enabled in AOS-CX, multicast traffic is selectively forwarded only to VTEPs connected to have interested hosts. This is achieved by exhanging IGMP reports over VXLAN tunnels where each VTEP processes the remote reports and uses the membership information to forward multicast traffic efficiently, conserving fabric bandwidth. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping vxlan replicate-all.

syntax: no ip igmp snooping vxlan replicate-all

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show ip igmp snooping command do?

The show ip igmp snooping command shows IGMP snooping configuration information and status for all VLANs. Specify a VLAN ID or a VLAN and a group to display details for only that VLAN or VLAN group. Shows IGMP query packets transmitted (Tx), received (Rx), and error packet counters. Shows IGMP Snooping details for all VLANs, including joined po rts or VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) for each group in the V LAN. Shows IGMP snooping groups information. Include the optional vlan <vlan-id> parameter to display information for groups o n a specific VLAN. Negates any configured parameter. packet-exceptions Troubleshoot issues in L2 multicast bridge entries for data pac kets forwarded to the CPU. Syntax: show ip igmp snooping 731.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred command do?

The ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred command enables the IPv6-only-preferred mode on the VLAN interface, and is also supported on the OOBM interface. Once enabled, the DHCP client prioritizes a IPv6 address over an IPv4 address. To obtain a DHCPv4 address, the IPv6-only-preferred command must be disabled, and the DHCPv4 client must be restarted. This command is disabled by default. NOTE Ensure the server supports the IPv6-only-preferred mode option, as the command will not work unless supported by the server. Public ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred 838 The no form of this command disables IPv6-only-preferred mode. NOTE The IPv6-only-preferred mode command must be configured prior to the ip dhcp command, otherwise it will not take effect. Syntax: no ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 address <global-unicast-address> command do?

The IPv6 address <global-unicast-address> command sets a global unicast address on the interface. The no form of this command removes the global unicast address on the interface. NOTE This command automatically creates an IPv6 link-local address on the interface. However, it does not add the IPv6 address link-local command to the running configuration. If you remove the IPv6 address, the link-local address is also removed. To maintain the link-local address, you must manually execute the IPv6 address link-local command. Syntax: no IPv6 address <global-unicast-address>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command do?

The IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command controls the M fLAGsetting in RAs the router transmits on the current interface. Enable the M flag to indicate that hosts can obtain IP address through DHCPv6. The M flag is disabled by default. The no form of this command turns off (disables) the M flag. 0 0 0 1 Indicates that no information is available via DHCPv6. Indicates that other configuratio n information is available via DH CPv6. Examples of such informat ion are DNS-related informatio n or information on other servers within the network. Public IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag 857 M flag O flag 1 1 0 1 Indicates that addresses are avail able via Dynamic Host Configura tion Protocol (DHCPv6). If the M flag is set, the O flag is redundant and can be ignored be cause DHCPv6 will return all avai lable configuration information. Syntax: no IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 nd ra max-interval command do?

The IPv6 nd ra max-interval command configures the maximum interval between transmissions of IPv6 RAs on the interface. The interval between RA transmissions on an interface is a random value that changes every time an RA is sent. The interval is calculated to be a value between the current max-interval and min-interval settings. The no form of this command returns the setting to its default, provided the default value is less than the default lifetime value. Specifies the maximum advertisement time in seconds. Range: 4-1800. Default: 600 seconds. <TIME> Configures the minimum interval between transmissions of IPv6 RAs on the interface. The interval between RA transmissions on an interface is a random value that changes every time an RA is sent. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra max-interval <TIME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command do?

The IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command adds static IPv6 client source binding information to the switch IPv6 binding database. Although DHCPv6 snooping is often used to dynamically populate the binding database, this command is available for manually adding entries to the switch IPv6 binding database. NOTE Statically configured IPv6 binding information supersedes any dynamically collected binding information for the same client. The no form of this command removes the specified binding that was statically configured with the IPv6 source-binding command. The no form has no effect on bindings that were dynamically configured with DHCPv6 snooping. Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094. <VLAN-ID> <IPv6-ADDR> <MAC-ADDR> Specifies the client IPv6 address. Syntax: no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip irdp holdtime <TIME> command do?

The ip irdp holdtime <TIME> command specifies the maximum amount of time the host will consider an advertisement to be valid until a newer advertisement arrives. When a new advertisement arrives, hold time is reset. Hold time must be greater than or equal to the maximum advertisement interval. Therefore, if the hold time for an advertisement expires, the host can reasonably conclude that the router interface that sent the advertisement is no longer available. The default hold time is three times the maximum advertisement interval. The no form of this command removes the specified maximum amount of time the host will consider an advertisement to be valid until a newer advertisement arrives and update it to the default value. Specifies the lifetime of router advertisements sent from this in terface. Range: 4 to 9000 seconds. Syntax: no ip irdp holdtime <TIME>.

The ip irdp maxadvertinterval <TIME> command specifies the maximum router advertisement interval. The no form of this command removes the specified maximum router advertisement interval and reverts to the default value. Specifies the maximum time allowed between the sending of u nsolicited router advertisements. Range: 4 to 1800 seconds. De fault: 600 seconds. <TIME>. Syntax: no ip irdp maxadvertinterval <TIME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip irdp minadvertinterval <TIME> command do?

The ip irdp minadvertinterval <TIME> command specifies the minimum amount of time the switch waits between sending router advertisements. By default, this value is automatically set by the switch to be 75% of the value configured for maximum router advertisement interval. Use this command to override the automatically configured value. The no form of this command removes the specified minimum amount of time the switch waits between sending router advertisements and reverts to the default value. Public ip irdp minadvertinterval 897 Specifies the minimum time allowed between the sending of u nsolicited router advertisements. Range: 3 to 1800 seconds. De fault: 450 seconds (75% of the default value for maximum route r advertisement interval). <TIME>. Syntax: no ip irdp minadvertinterval <TIME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show lldp local-device command do?

The show lldp local-device command shows global LLDP information advertised by the switch, as well as port-based data. If VLANs are configured on any active interfaces, the VLAN ID is only shown for trunk native or untagged VLAN IDs on access interfaces. Displays information about neighboring devices for all interfaces or for a specific interface. The information displayed varies depending on the type of neighbor connected and the type of TLVs sent by the neighbor. <INTERFACE-NAME> Specifies the interface for which to show information for neigh boring devices. Use the format member/slot/port (for example, 1/3/1). Shows detailed LLDP neighbor information for all LLDP neighbor connected interfaces. Syntax: show lldp local-device.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show lldp neighbor-info json command do?

The show lldp neighbor-info json command displays LLDP neighbor information in structured JSON format. This command provides the same information as the standard show lldp neighbor-info command, but it prints the output in JSON format. Shows global LLDP statistics or statistics for a specific interface. Specifies an interface. Format: member/slot/port. <INTERFACE-ID> Shows the LLDP TLVs that are configured for send and receive. Defines accounting as being local (with the name local) (the default). Or defines a sequence of remote AAA server groups to be accessed for accounting purposes. For remote accounting, the information is sent to the first reachable remote server that was configured with this command for remote accounting. If no remote server is reachable, local accounting remains available. Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info json.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators command do?

The aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators command enables/disables privilege elevation for the users in an administrators group. When enabled, an administrative user is provided lower privilege access upon successful switch login. The user must execute an enable command and re-authenticate to elevate their privilege to administrator (the administrator level Public aaa authentication login privilege-elevation 1047 access with ‘#’ prompt). Executing the disable command revokes an administrator privilege and returns the user to the lower privilege access level (the operator level access ‘>’ prompt). The no form of this command disables privilege elevation for the administrator user group. Syntax: no aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators.

command: aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authorization commands <CONNECTION-TYPE> group <GROUP-LIST> command do?

The aaa authorization commands <CONNECTION-TYPE> group <GROUP-LIST> command defines authorization as being basic local RBAC (specified as none), or as full-fledged local RBAC specified as local (the default), or as remote TACACS+ (specified with group <GROUP-LIST>). Each available connection type (channel) can be configured individually. All server groups named in the command, must exist. This command can be issued multiple times, once for each connection type. The no form of this command unconfigures authorization for the specified connection type, reverting to the default of local. NOTE Although only TACACS+ servers are supported for remote authorization, local authorization (basic or full-fledged) can be used with remote RADIUS authentication. Syntax: no aaa authorization commands <CONNECTION-TYPE> group <GROUP-LIST>.

If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show aaa accounting.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show loop-protect command do?

The show loop-protect command this command shows the following global configurations. • Transmit interval. • Re-enable timer. • Per-port configurations. • Loop-protect enable or disable status. • Loop detection. • Loop detected count. • Timestamp of latest loop detection. • Loop is detected on VLAN. • Interface status. • List of configured VLAN's for that port. • VTEP port information Specify the interface name on display for the filter. When rebooting the switch or after switchover, The loop-detected count on the loop detected port is reset to zero. <INTERFACE-NAME> • An Ethernet interface associated with a physical port. Form Specifies the name of a logical interface on the switch. This can be one of the following: at: member/slot/port. • A LAG (link aggregation group). Specify the ID of LAG. Syntax: show loop-protect [<INTERFACE-NAME>].

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show mac-address-table static command do?

The show mac-address-table static command shows all statically configured MAC addresses. Shows MAC addresses learned by or configured on the specified VLAN. Specifies the VLAN ID. Displays the MAC entries in the MAC address table that have moved at least once. The output can be filtered based on a specific VLAN or specific MAC address and VLAN. Displays information for a specific MAC address. <address> <vlan> Adds a static MAC address to the MAC address table and associates it with a port or existing VLAN. Static MAC addresses can only be assigned to layer 2 (non-routed) interfaces. Static MAC addresses are not affected by the MAC address aging time. The no form of this command deletes a static MAC address. Specifies a MAC address (xx: xx: xx: xx: xx: xx), where x is a hexa decimal number from 0 to F. Syntax: show mac-address-table static.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping command this command configures the drop unknown mode. While MLD snooping is enabled, the traffic will be forwarded only to ports that initiate an MLD request for multicast. Drop unknown mode can be a filter across all VLANs (vlan-shared) or per VLAN (exclusive-vlan). The default configuration is vlan-shared. The no form of this command configures the drop unknown mode on the switch to the default vlan-shared. Required: Enable shared VLAN filter on the switch. vlan-exclusive Required: Enable exclusive drop unknown filter per VLAN. Public MLD snooping global configuration commands 1135 This command disables or reenables MLD snooping on the VLAN. Starting with AOS-CX 10.16, MLD snooping is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables all MLD snooping configurations on the VLAN. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping drop-unknown {vlan-shared | vlan-exclusive}.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the MLD join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. Public IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list 1138 The no form of this command disables the rules set for the ACL. NOTE This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Associates an ACL with the IGMP. Specifies the name of the ACL. <ACL-NAME> NOTE If the access list is configured for both L2 VLA N and L3 VLAN, the L3 VLAN configuration will be applied. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command enable MLD snooping on all VLANs. This configuration setting enables MLD snooping on all existing VLANs, as well as on any VLANs that are created after the IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command is issued. When MLD snooping is enabled globally, MLD snooping can not be configured on individual VLANs within the VLAN context of the command-line interface. The no form of this command disables global MLD snooping, allows MLD snooping to remain enabled at the VLAN level, and allows the configuration of VLAN-level MLD snooping settings. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans.

The IPv6 mld snooping [auto vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command this command configures the given ports in auto mode, which is the default port mode. The no form of this command disables auto ports. Required: Specifies a list of VLANs on which the port should be configured as an auto port. Specifies the number of a single VL AN or a series of numbers for a range of VLANs, separated by c ommas (10, 20, 30, 40), dashes (10-40), or both (10-40, 60). Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping [auto vlan <VLAN-LIST>].

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping [blocked vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping [blocked vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command by default ports are configured in auto mode. This command configures the given ports in blocked mode. The no form of this command removes blocked ports. Required: Specifies a list of VLANs on which the port should be configured as a blocked port. Specifies the number of a single V LAN or a series of numbers for a range of VLANs, separated by commas (10, 20, 30, 40), dashes (10-40), or both (10-40, 6 0). This command enables the port to learn group information on receiving topology change notification. The no form of this command disables fastlearn on the ports. Required: 1/1/1-1/1/2, ports to be configured as fastlearn por ts. Public IPv6 mld snooping fastlearn 1144. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping fastlearn <port-list>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping [fastleave vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping [fastleave vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command configures the specified ports as fastleave ports. Enables the switch to immediately remove an interface from the bridge table upon receiving the leave group message. The no form of this command disables fastleave configuration on the ports. Public IPv6 mld snooping fastleave vlan 1145 Required: Specifies a list of VLANs on which the port should be configured as a fastleave port. Specifies the number of a single VLAN or a series of numbers for a range of VLANs, separated by commas (10, 20, 30, 40), dashes (10-40), or both (10-40, 60). MLD fastleave is configured for ports on a per-VLAN basis. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping [fastleave vlan <VLAN-LIST>].

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping [forward vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping [forward vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command by default ports are configured in auto mode. This command configures the given ports in forward mode. The no form of this command disables forward ports. Required: Specifies a list of VLANs on which the port should be configured as a forward port. Specifies the number of a single V LAN or a series of numbers for a range of VLANs, separated by commas (10, 20, 30, 40), dashes (10-40), or both (10-40, 6 0). This command configures the preprogramming of the starg (*, G) flow feature on MLD snooping enabled VLANs. Enable preprogramming (*, G) flows on the VLAN. Disable prprogramming (*, G) flows on the VLAN. When this feature is enabled, a summarized multicast bridge entry is programmed into the hardware table when a (*, G) or sg MLD join is received on the MLD snooping enabled VLAN. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping preprogram-starg-flow {enable | disable}.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all command this command enables AOS-CX leaf devices (access-layer switches that connect directly to servers, storage, and other endpoints) to unconditionally forward multicast traffic to all VTEPs that do not support the exchange of MLD reports over the fabric. By default, when MLD snooping is enabled in AOS-CX, multicast traffic is selectively forwarded only to VTEPs connected to have interested hosts. This is achieved by exhanging MLD reports over VXLAN tunnels where each VTEP processes the remote reports and uses the membership information to forward multicast traffic efficiently, conserving fabric bandwidth. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all.

syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld version <VERSION> command do?

The IPv6 mld version <VERSION> command this command configures MLD version. The no form of the command configures the default MLD version of 2. Required: 1-2, configures MLD version. <VERSION> This command configures MLD strict version. Packets that do not match the configured version will be dropped. By default, strict option is not enabled. Required: 1-2, configures MLD version. <VERSION>. Syntax: IPv6 mld version <VERSION> [strict].

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> command do?

The show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> command displays MSTP configurations for the given instance ID. Specifies the MSTP instance number. Range: 0 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID> Displays MSTP configurations for the given instance ID with corresponding port details. Specifies the MSTP instance number. Range: 0 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID> Public show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail 1214 Shows MSTP configurations for the given instance ID with corresponding port details. Public show spanning-tree mst interface 1216 Specifies the MSTP instance number. Range: 0 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID> <IFNAME> Shows spanning tree port summary information. Shows spanning tree root summary information. Enables the spanning tree protocol on the switch. The no form of this command disables the spanning tree protocol on the switch. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID> command do?

The ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID> command sets a key as trusted. When NTP authentication is enabled, the switch only synchronizes with time servers that transmit packets containing a trusted key. The no form of this command removes the trusted designation from a key. Specifies the identification number of the key to set as trusted. Range: 1 to 65534. <KEY-ID>. Syntax: no ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf network command do?

The ip ospf network command configures the OSPFv2 network type for the interface. The no form of this command sets the network type for the interface to the system default which is broadcast network. NOTE For an IP unnumbered interface, the network type should be configured as a point-to-point. Specifies the OSPFv2 network type as a broadcast multi-acc ess network. Specifies the OSPFv2 network type as a point-to-point netw ork. Specifies the OSPFv2 network type as a point-to-multipoint net work. Supports non-broadcast mode (static neighbor configura tion) and broadcast mode (dynamic neighbor discovery with dy namic option). NOTE It is recommended to configure the same network type on all OSPFv2 peers to ensure proper route learning and adjacency formation. Syntax: ip ospf network {broadcast | point-to-point | point-to-multipoint[dynamic]}.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf passive command do?

The ip ospf passive command configures the interface as an OSPF passive interface. With this setting, the interface participates in OSPF but does not send or receive packets on that interface. The no form of this command resets the interface as active. With this setting, the interface starts sending and receiving OSPF packets. Syntax:
- no ip ospf passive
- ip ospf passive 1314.

syntax:
- no ip ospf passive
- ip ospf passive 1314

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the neighbor <neighbor-ip> command do?

The neighbor <neighbor-ip> command this command configures a static OSPFv2 neighbor for point-to-multipoint networks. Static neighbors are required for NBMA (Non-Broadcast Multiple Access) networks, where automatic neighbor discovery through multicast hello packets is not possible. The neighbor IP address should be the IP address of the interface on the neighboring router within the same network segment. The no form of this command removes a previously configured static OSPFv2 neighbor from the point-to- multipoint network configuration. neighbor <neighbor-ip> Static neighbor IP address for point-to-multipoint networks. Syntax:
- no neighbor <neighbor-ip>
- neighbor 1322.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME> command do?

The crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME> command configures an application to associate with a user-configured certificate. By default, all applications are associated with the default certificate "local-cert". Public crypto pki application 1343 Note that applications "captive-portal" and "hsc" are only available on certain platforms. The certificate associated with EST client is global to all EST profiles. Among the available applications, only dot1x-supplicant, est-client, and radsec-client are allowed to associate with device-identity certificate. Also note that device-identity certificate on a switch using a TPM of legacy version 1.2 only works with TLS version 1.2. 1. Syntax: no crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command do?

The crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command creates a leaf certificate and changes to its context config-cert-<CERT-NAME>. If the specified leaf certificate exists, this command changes to its context. The first time the switch starts it creates a self-signed, default leaf certificate called local-cert. This certificate is used by any switch application that does not have an associated leaf certificate. The no form of this command deletes the specified leaf certificate. The default leaf certificate local-cert cannot be deleted. Specifies the name of a leaf certificate. Range: 1 to 32 alphanu meric characters (excluding "). <CERT-NAME>. Syntax: no crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show crypto pki application command do?

The show crypto pki application command shows certificate information for all features (applications) using leaf certificates that are managed by PKI. Shows a list of all configured leaf certificates, or detailed information for a specific leaf certificate. Possible values for Cert Status are: CSR pending, expired, expires soon, installed, malformed, not yet known. Possible values for EST Status are: enroll failed, enroll pending, enroll retrying, enroll success, n/a (certificate is not EST-enrolled), reenroll failed, reenroll pending, reenroll retrying. Public show crypto pki certificate 1370 Specifies the leaf certificate name. Range: 1 to 32 alphanumeric characters excluding ". <CERT-NAME> plaintext pem Shows a list of all configured TA profiles, or detailed information for a specific profile. Syntax: show crypto pki application.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius command configures the authentication mechanism used to control access to the network. The configured authentication method will be used to authenticate 802.1X clients. The no form of the command resets the authentication mechanism to the default, eap-radius. Specifies the EAP RADIUS as the 802.1X authentication meth od. Public aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator... 1422 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s d o t 1 x a u t h e n t i c a t o r... Enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows 802.1X reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users already authenticated retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables the cached reauthentication on a port. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth.

command: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius
syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth-period command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth-period command configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 30 seconds. Specifies the cached reauthentication period (in seconds). Defa ult: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD> Configures the period the port waits to retransmit the next EAPOL request identity frame on an 802.1X enabled port that has no authenticated clients. The no form of the command resets the discovery period to the default, 30 seconds. Specifies the discovery period (in seconds). Default: 30. Range: 1 to 65535. <PERIOD> Configure the maximum size in bytes of an EAP-TLS fragment encoded in a single RADIUS request packet. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator discovery-period <PERIOD>.

command: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth-period

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator radius server-group command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator radius server-group command configures the switch to use an existing RADIUS server group for 802.1X authentication globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the server group to the default, radius. Public aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator... 1435 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s d o t 1 x a u t h e n t i c a t o r... When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator radius server-group.

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth command enables periodic reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic reauthentication. Public aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator... 1437 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s d o t 1 x a u t h e n t i c a t o r... Configures the period after which the authenticated clients are reauthenticated on the port. You must enable reauthentication on the port before configuring the reauthentication period. Public aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator... 1438 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s d o t 1 x a u t h e n t i c a t o r... The no form of the command resets the reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the reauthentication period (in seconds). Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows MAC reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users who are already authenticated, retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables cached reauthentication. Configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-rea... 1551 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h c a c h e d - r e a... Specifies the cached reauthentication period (in seconds). Defa ult: 3600. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME> command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME> command configures the MAC authentication server group globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the authentication server group to the default value, radius. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius ser... 1555 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h r a d i u s s e r... When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth command enables periodic MAC reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic MAC reauthentication on the port. Configures the period after which MAC authenticated clients must be reauthenticated on the port. You must first enable MAC reauthentication on the port before configuring the MAC reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the MAC reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the MAC reauthentication period (in seconds). Default: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth-per... 1558 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h r e a u t h - p e r. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos egress-rewrite-map local-priority <PRIORITY-RANGE> {cos <PCP-VALUE>} command do?

The qos egress-rewrite-map local-priority <PRIORITY-RANGE> {cos <PCP-VALUE>} command configures a system-wide mapping from internal local priority to an 802.1Q VLAN priority code point (PCP) or DSCP value. The qos egress-rewrite cos command allows the PCP value to be rewritten on egress, and the qos egress-rewrite dscp command allows the DSCP value to be rewritten on egress. The local priority associated with a packet affects only its behavior within the switch. By default, it does not influence the priority value written to the packet upon transmission. This command, along with its no form, accepts a single local priority, a range of local priorities, or a comma-separated list of local priorities and ranges. This provides a convenient method for configuring multiple entries in the map. Syntax: no qos egress-rewrite-map local-priority <PRIORITY-RANGE> {cos <PCP-VALUE>}.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos queue-profile <NAME> command do?

The qos queue-profile <NAME> command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile.. A queue profile maps queues to CoS values. Each profile has two, four, or eight queues numbered 0 to 7. The larger the queue number, the higher its priority during transmission scheduling. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS queue profile. Only profiles that are not currently applied can be removed. Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). <NAME>. Syntax: no qos queue-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos schedule-profile <NAME> command do?

The qos schedule-profile <NAME> command creates a QoS schedule profile and switches to the config-schedule context for the profile. If the specified schedule profile exists, this command switches to the config-schedule context for the profile. The schedule profile determines the order in which queues are selected to transmit a packet, and the amount of service defined for each queue. Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). <NAME>. Syntax: no qos schedule-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show qos threshold-profile command do?

The show qos threshold-profile command shows the status of all threshold profiles, or a specific threshold profile. Specifies the name of a threshold profile. Range: 1 to 64 alphan umeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyph en (-). <NAME> Shows the global QoS trust settings, or the factory default settings. Shows the factory default QoS trust settings. Public show qos trust 1701 Assigns the strict priority algorithm to a queue. Strict priority services all packets waiting in a queue, before servicing the packets in lower priority queues. Egress queue shaping can be configured using the max-bandwidth option to limit the amount of traffic transmitted per output queue. The buffer associated with each egress queue stores the excess traffic to Public strict queue 1702 smooth the output rate. Syntax: show qos threshold-profile [<NAME>].

Specifies a VLAN ID number. <VLAN-ID> Shows a summary of port-related spanning-tree configuration and status. Shows the summary of spanning tree root and configurations for all VLANs. Displays the spanning tree mode and information on the RPVST instance of the specified VLAN. Specifies the number of a VLAN. <VLAN-ID> Displays the spanning tree mode and information on the RPVST instance of the specified VLAN and optionally displays details on the RPVST instance for the VLAN. Specifies the number of a VLAN. Public show spanning-tree vlan detail 1862 Enables and configures the auto re-enable timeout in seconds for all interfaces with BPDU guard enabled. messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show rmon alarm command do?

The show rmon alarm command displays the RMON alarm configurations. Specifies the RMON alarm index. Range: 1 to 20. Displays SNMP agent UDP port number. Displays a list of all configured SNMP v1/v2c communities. Displays all configured SNMP contexts. Displays the configured SNMP v3 snmp engine-id. If the SNMP v3 engine-id is not configured, by default a unique engine-id is created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Displays the configured SNMP v3 security level. Displays SNMP description, location, and contact information. Displays all configured SNMP traps/informs receivers. Displays all configured SNMP v3 users. For more details on the user enabled status, see snmpv3 security-level. Displays the list of all the configured SNMP views. Syntax: show rmon alarm [index <INDEX>].

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server community <STRING> command do?

The snmp-server community <STRING> command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is like a password that controls read/ write access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. <STRING> Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Syntax: no snmp-server community <STRING>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server snmpv3-only command do?

The snmp-server snmpv3-only command accepts SNMP v3 messages only, SNMP v1 and SNMP v2c will be disabled. By default SNMP v1, SNMP v2c and SNMP v3 will all be enabled. The no form of this command restores the default setting and reenables SNMP v1 and SNMP v2c. Syntax: no snmp-server snmpv3-only.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status command do?

The snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status command enables the SNMP trap for AAA server status. When enabled, traps are sent whenever AAA server (RADIUS, TACACS) status changes from reachable to unreachable and vice versa. The no form of this command disables sending SNMP trap for AAA server status. Syntax: no snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server trap mac-notify command do?

The snmp-server trap mac-notify command enables the MAC notification traps within the SNMP module at a global level. When enabled, traps are sent for interfaces that are configured for MAC notification events. The no form of this command disables sending MAC notification traps at a global level. When disabled, existing mac-notify interface configuration is preserved but MAC notification events on configured interfaces will not cause SNMP traps to be transmitted. Syntax: no snmp-server trap mac-notify.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ssh server vrf <VRF-NAME> command do?

The ssh server vrf <VRF-NAME> command enables the SSH server on the specified VRF. SSH is disabled by default and will not be operational till the admin password is set on the switch. Note that the admin password is considered set even if it is configured to be empty. The no form of the command disables the SSH server on the specified VRF. If no VRF is specified, by default the SSH server will be enabled on the default or mgmt VRF, depending on the switch model. Public ssh server vrf 2056 Specifies the VRF name. Syntax: no ssh server vrf <VRF-NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR> command adds an IPv4 static route on the default VRF. The no form of this command deletes a IPv4 static route. NOTE You can configure a maximum of 32 next hops per route. Specifies the IPv4 route destination. <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> Public ip route 2061 Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 32. <NETMASK> <NEXTHOP-ADDR> <NEXTHOP-PORT-LAG-VLAN> nullroute reject Specifies the next hop address for reaching the destination in I Pv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 25 5. Specifies the next hop as an outgoing interface. Specifies that packets matching the destination route are silentl y discarded and no ICMP error notification is sent to the sender. Syntax: no ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command do?

The ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command enables SSH enforcement that the username must be present in the certificate that is being used for authorization. This configuration alters how certificate-based authentication maps to a user account. When this is enabled, SSH will not require local user association with an authorized-key and instead enforces that the username used to log in is present within the certificate. The SSH server will check for the username in certificate fields Common Name or User Principle Name for a match. If a certificate is not used for authentication then this configuration has no effect on SSH authentication. The no form of this command disables the SSH enforcement of username in the certificate. Public ssh certificate-as-authorized-key 2283. Syntax: no ssh certificate-as-authorized-key.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vlan <VLAN-LIST> command do?

The vlan <VLAN-LIST> command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the shutdown command. If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. NOTE VLANs used for internal purposes using the command system internal vlan range cannot be used for any other (L2) purposes. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted. Specifies a single ID, or a series of IDs separated by commas (2, 3, 4), dashes (2-4), or both (2-4, 6). Range: 1 to. A maximu m of 512 (6000, 6100 Switch Series) VLANs are supported. <VLAN-LIST>. Syntax: no vlan <VLAN-LIST>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vlan access command do?

The vlan access command creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned to each access interface. VLANs can only be assigned to non-routed (Layer 2) interfaces. All interfaces are non-routed (Layer 2) by default when created. Use routing and no routing commands to move ports between Layer 3 and Layer 2 interfaces. The no form of this command removes an access VLAN from the interface in the current context and sets it to the default VLAN ID of 1. Specifies a single ID, or a series of IDs separated by commas (2, 3, 4), dashes (2-4), or both (2-4, 6). Range: 1 to. A maximu m of 512 (6000, 6100 Switch Series) VLANs are supported. <VLAN-ID> Assigns a VLAN ID to an trunk interface. Multiple VLAN IDs can be assigned to a trunk interface. Syntax: vlan access <VLAN-ID>.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, how do you configure CoPP?

To configure CoPP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the default CoPP policy, edit an existing policy, or create a policy with the command copp-p 2. Add, edit, or remove classes in the policy with the command class 3. Review the CoPP policy configuration settings with the command show copp-policy.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the apply copp-policy command do?

The apply copp-policy command applies a CoPP policy to the switch, replacing the policy that is in effect. There may be a brief interruption in traffic flow to the management processor while the switch implements the change. Enter the no apply copp-policy <NAME> command with the name of a CoPP policy to unapply a CoPP policy and apply the default CoPP policy. This will only take effect if the specified policy is actively applied. Since there must always be a CoPP policy applied, this command effectively attempts to replace the applied CoPP policy with the default CoPP policy. For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the diag event-trap-disable command do?

The diag event-trap-disable command prevents the specific event notifications from being sent as traps to the SNMP management stations. The no form of this command disables this feature and reenables the notification of events to be sent as traps. Specify up to five comma separated Event IDs To modify the traps to be disabled, such as changing the existing list of event IDs or removing an event ID from the blocked trap list, first completely remove previously configured disabled traps using the CLI command no diag event-trap-disable <EVENT-ID>. After that, reconfigure the new event IDs or set of event IDs for the changes to take effect. The command to disable traps cannot be issued via network management systems such as SNMP, the REST API, HPE Aruba Networking Fabric Composer or HPE Aruba Networking Central. Syntax: diag event-trap-disable <EVENT-IDS>.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about IPv6 Example?

IPv6 Example: switch(config)# logrotate target tftp://2001: db8: 0: 1: : 128 Remote transfer of rotated log files Only the TFTP protocol is supported for remote transfer, and both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses are supported. Only newly rotated log files are transferred to the remote host during the log rotation. Previously rotated log files are not transferred. After a file is successfully transferred, it is removed from the switch local path. Packet level failures with TFTP are handled in the protocol itself. With each TFTP session failure, TFTP retries the file transfer three times. Retries have a timeout of five seconds.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the logrotate period {daily | hourly | monthly | weekly} command do?

The logrotate period {daily | hourly | monthly | weekly} command sets the log file rotation time period. Defaults to daily. A log file that exceeds either the logrotate maxsize or the logrotate period (whichever happens first), triggers rotation of the log file. The no form of this command resets the log rotation period to the default of daily. Rotates log files on a daily basis (default) at 0: 01. Rotates log files every hour at the first second of the hour. Rotates log files monthly on the first day of the month at 00: 01. Rotates log files once a week on Sunday at 00: 01. Setting a weekly period: switch(config)# logrotate period weekly Resetting the period to its default of daily: switch(config)# no logrotate period. Syntax: logrotate period {daily | hourly | monthly | weekly}.

Syntax: logrotate period {daily | hourly | monthly | weekly}

To clear accounting logs on switches with this feature enabled, users should first revert the native accounting format back to the default AOS-CX format by executing the no logging accounting-format-native command. The logging command enables syslog forwarding to a remote syslog server. The no form of this command disables syslog forwarding to a remote syslog server. Starting with AOS-CX 10.11, payload information is present in accounting logs. The maximum REST payload that can be sent to RADIUS/TACACS server is 1024 characters, and the maximum of REST payload that can be sent to syslog server is 3500 characters. If this limit is is reached, the log will display three dots (...) to indicate that the log an exceeded the character limit and is incomplete. For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the system serviceos password-prompt command do?

The system serviceos password-prompt command use this command to enable password authentication for ServiceOS. By default, the ServiceOS shell (accessible only from the local switch console port) requires no password to login as an admin use. When this setting is enabled, the same password used to authenticate the admin user in the AOS-CX CLI or WebUI can be used to log in to the ServiceOS shell. If this setting is enabled, a forgotten admin user password cannot be reset using ServiceOS; if there are no other local or RADIUS/TACACS user accounts with administrator-level access, the switch must be zeroized by entering the username zeroize command at the ServiceOS login prompt to restore administrator access. serviceos password-prompt cannot be enabled if the built-in admin user has been disabled. The troubleshoot multicast command use the troubleshoot multicast commands to troubleshoot multicast networks while focusing on specific multicast settings. This targeted approach to troubleshooting helps identify and fix issues in large, complex networks that include multiple virtual routing domains (VRFs) and interfaces. AOS-CX troubleshooting works with both IPv4 and IPv6 multicast protocols, and can address multicast group membership problems with IGMP and MLD, and verify the routing paths for multicast traffic using PIM or PIM6.
