For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the troubleshoot multicast command?
Syntax: troubleshoot multicast
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the troubleshoot radius tracking health [verbose] command?
Syntax: troubleshoot radius tracking health [verbose]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the troubleshoot system command do?

The troubleshoot system command perform basic troubleshooting for system-level components. capacity-check Perform diagnostic checks on system capacities and their valu es. interfaces resources health [verbose] Perform diagnostic checks on physical interfaces. Perform diagnostic checks on system CPU and memory utilizati on. Perform diagnostic checks on the status of system daemons/ser vices. Display basic information for system errors, including the error message, message ID, severity level, and when the message was last reported. The troubleshoot system health command disp lays troubleshooting information for all system health compone nts. Include the health parameter after a specific system compo nent name to display basic troubleshooting information for that component only. Syntax: troubleshoot system.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the troubleshoot system command?
Syntax: troubleshoot system
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show arp vlan command do?

The show arp vlan command displays IPv4 neighbors learnt on L2 VLAN interfaces only when arp-suppression-extended is enabled. Specifies the VLAN ID. Shows information for all VLANs. If global arp-suppression-extended is enabled and per-VLAN arp-suppression-extended is disabled, neighbors learnt on that VLAN are not displayed. If global arp-suppression-extended is disabled and per-VLAN arp-suppression-extended is enabled, neighbors learnt on that VLAN are displayed. Syntax: show arp vlan {VLAN-ID|all}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show arp vlan command?
Syntax: show arp vlan {VLAN-ID|all}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show evpn evi command do?
The show evpn evi command shows the information of EVPN instances. Syntax: show evpn evi.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi command?
Syntax: show evpn evi
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show evpn evi summary command do?
The show evpn evi summary command shows the summary information for EVPN instances. Syntax: show evpn evi summary.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi summary command?
Syntax: show evpn evi summary
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> command do?
The show evpn evi <EVI-ID> command shows the information for the particular EVPN instance. Specifies the EVPN instance ID. <EVI-ID>. Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> command?
Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail command do?
The show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail command shows the detailed information for the particular EVPN instance. Specifies the EVPN instance ID. <EVI-ID>. Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail command?
Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show evpn evi igmp snooping command do?

The show evpn evi igmp snooping command shows the information about the IGMP group memberships learned through EVPN. A default SMET route is identified by the Source and Group addresses set to 0.0.0.0 and is applicable for both IP versions. Specifies the EVPN instance ID. Range: 1-16777214. Public show evpn evi igmp snooping 197. Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> igmp snooping.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi igmp snooping command?
Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> igmp snooping
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show evpn mac-ip command do?
The show evpn mac-ip command show the information about the EVPN MAC-IP for the EVPN instances. Syntax: show evpn mac-ip.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show evpn mac-ip command?
Syntax: show evpn mac-ip
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show evpn mac-ip evi command do?
The show evpn mac-ip evi command show the information about the EVPN MAC-IP for the particular EVPN instance. Specifies the EVPN instance ID. <EVI-ID>. Syntax: show evpn mac-ip evi <EVI-ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show evpn mac-ip evi command?
Syntax: show evpn mac-ip evi <EVI-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show evpn evi mld snooping command do?

The show evpn evi mld snooping command shows the information about the MLD group memberships learned through EVPN. A default SMET route is identified by the Source and Group addresses set to 0: : 0 and is applicable for both IP versions. Specifies the EVPN instance ID. Range: 1-16777214. <EVI-ID>. Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> mld snooping.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi mld snooping command?
Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> mld snooping
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show evpn vtep-neighbor command do?

The show evpn vtep-neighbor command shows the remote VTEPs MAC-IP binding. The state of the peer VTEP denotes whether VXLAN tunnel to the VTEP is Up or Down. Display information for all VRFs. Specify a VRF by VRF name (if no <VRF-NAME> is specifie d, the default VRF is implied. Syntax: show evpn vtep-neighbor {all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-Name>}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show evpn vtep-neighbor command?
Syntax: show evpn vtep-neighbor {all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-Name>}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear dsm statistics command do?

The clear dsm statistics command clears the statistics of the interfaces attached to the distributed services module. Shows the slot ID of the DSM. <SLOT-ID> global Clears the DSM interface statistics for all sessions. Public DSM commands 760. Syntax: clear dsm <SLOT-ID> statistics [global].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the clear dsm statistics command?
Syntax: clear dsm <SLOT-ID> statistics [global]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the diag dsm console command do?

The diag dsm console command provides access to the operator console of the attached distributed services module. From this console, operator commands can be run. Exiting the console returns the user back to CLI. This is intended for diagnostic purposes only. Public diag dsm console 761 Shows the slot ID of the DSM. <SLOT-ID>. Syntax: diag dsm console <SLOT-ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the diag dsm console command?
Syntax: diag dsm console <SLOT-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show dsm command?
Syntax: show dsm <SLOT-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show dsm erspan command do?
The show dsm erspan command displays the status of the Encapsulated Remote Switched Port Analyzer (ERSPAN) feature for DSM. Public show dsm erspan 768. Syntax: show dsm erspan.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show dsm erspan command?
Syntax: show dsm erspan
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the interface vxlan command do?

The interface vxlan command creates VXLAN interface 1 and changes to the config-vxlan-if context. A maximum of one VXLAN interface is supported. By default, the VXLAN is disabled. To enable the VXLAN, use the command no shutdown. The no form of this command removes VXLAN interface 1. This deletes the VXLAN tunnel, and all VNIs and VLAN-to-VNI mappings associated with it. Only one VXLAN interface is supported. (Optional) Specify if the interface tunnel uses IPv4 or IPv6 add ressing. If this parameter is not included, the interface default is IPv6. Syntax: interface vxlan 1 [mode IPv4|IPv6].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the interface vxlan command?
Syntax: interface vxlan 1 [mode IPv4|IPv6]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip-fragment-bypass command do?

The ip-fragment-bypass command this command toggles IP fragment bypass functionality for a VLAN. When enabled, IP fragment packets that are normally redirected to the Distributed Services Module, are not redirected. This is configured on a per-VLAN basis. Syntax: ip-fragment-bypass.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip-fragment-bypass command?
Syntax: ip-fragment-bypass
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show interface vxlan command?
Syntax: show interface vxlan <IFNAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show interface vxlan vni command do?

The show interface vxlan vni command shows detailed VNI information for the VXLAN interface. Specifies the number for a given VNI. Range: 1 to 16777214. Shows the VTEPS information for a specified VNI. transport-VRFshows the transport VRFstatus for a specified VNI. Syntax: show interface vxlan vni <VNI-ID> [vteps | transport-vrf].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show interface vxlan vni command?
Syntax: show interface vxlan vni <VNI-ID> [vteps | transport-vrf]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show vlan ip-fragment-bypass command do?

The show vlan ip-fragment-bypass command this command displays the status of VLANs with IP fragment bypass enabled. Disables the VXLAN interface and deletes all VXLAN tunnels, segments, and members on the interface. The no form of this command starts the VXLAN interface and creates all VXLAN tunnels and segments. If members are configured, they are added to the VXLAN segment. Configures the source IPv4 address for a VXLAN interface. All VXLAN encapsulated packets use this source IP address in the outer IP header. If you change an existing source IP address, all tunnels with the old source IP address are deleted, and new tunnels are created with the new source IP address. The no form of this command deletes the source IP address for the VXLAN interface and deletes all VXLAN tunnels using this source IP address. Syntax: show vlan ip-fragment-bypass.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show vlan ip-fragment-bypass command?
Syntax: show vlan ip-fragment-bypass

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is High Availability?

High Availability: Key goals of HA include: • Achieve five-nines (99.999%) availability of switching traffic through minimization of unplanned network outages. • Fault tolerant: No single active component failure will cause an outage. • Live replacement of hardware with minimal or no disruption.
Terminology: • MM: Abbreviation for management module • MM to MM link: Refers to the 10GbE-KR Ethernet link between two MMs • OVSDB: Abbreviation for Open vSwitch Database.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is Management Module Failover?

Management Module Failover: There are two types of Management Module (MM) failover: • Controlled failover: The user triggers this type of failover by rebooting the Active MM or running the r edundancy switchover command. • Uncontrolled failover: This type of failover is triggered by unexpected events like a crash on the Active MM or hot removal of the Active MM. In a dual MM chassis, the Standby MM detects failover events in one of the following ways: • A mailbox interrupt is received from the Active MM to indicate takeover. This interrupt can come for controlled or uncontrolled failover (except for a hot removal). • Active MM hot removal detection. • Heartbeat loss detected on the Standby MM for more than 10 seconds. NOTE If the Active MM is not responding and is still not detected by the first two methods, it will be caught by this method.
Failover requirements: • The Standby MM must be present to trigger a failover. An Unassigned MM will never trigger a failover. Public.

section: AAA on Switches with Multiple Management Modules

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about AAA on Switches with Multiple Management Modules?

AAA on Switches with Multiple Management Modules: Consider the following when working with local authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) on switches with multiple management modules: • Local authentication: ◦ The user database is synchronized between the Active and Standby management modules. ◦ Only local users belonging to the administrators group and using local password authentication are permitted to log in to the Standby management module. Alternatively, the Standby management module can be accessed from the Active management module by providing a logged in admin user password. • Local authorization: ◦ A few nonconfiguration commands are available on the Standby management module. ◦ For expert users, the bash shell is available on the Standby management module. • Local accounting: ◦ The audit logs used for local accounting are available only on the Active Management Module.

topic: AAA on Switches with Multiple Management Modules

BFD Features: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 Static Route Configuring BFD for BGP Configuring BFD for OSPFv2 Configuring BFD for OSPFv3 Configuring BFD for OSPFv3 AF Configuring BFD for PIM Over IPv4 Configuring BFD for PIM Over IPv6 Configuring BFD for VRRP.

section: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 Static Route

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, how do you configure BFD for an IPv4 Static Route?
To configure BFD for an IPv4 Static Route, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on an IPv4 static route with the command ip route bfd.

task: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 Static Route

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, how do you configure BFD for BGP?

To configure BFD for BGP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command 2. Create a BGP peer and initiate a connection to it with the command neighbor remote-as 3. Enable BFD on a BGP interface with the command neighbor fall-over bfd. Commands shown in the procedure include: bfd.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, how do you configure BFD for OSPFv3 AF?

To configure BFD for OSPFv3 AF, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD for all interfaces enabled for OSPFv3 AF by using the BFD all-interfaces command in 2. Enable BFD for a subset of interfaces that have OSPFv3 AF enabled by using the ospfv3-af bfd 3. Enable BFD support with the command bfd.

section: Configuring BFD for PIM Over IPv6

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, how do you configure BFD for PIM Over IPv6?

To configure BFD for PIM Over IPv6, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Switch to the interface on which you want to enable BFD with the command interface 3. Review BFD configuration settings with the commands show bfd. Commands shown in the procedure include: BFD setting | | Default value | Command to change it; BFD session flaps depending upon traffic conditions..

task: Configuring BFD for PIM Over IPv6

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, how do you configure BFD for VRRP?
To configure BFD for VRRP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on a VRRP interface with the command bfd <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the bfd command do?

The bfd command enables BFD support on the switch. BFD is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables BFD and removes all related configuration settings. To disable BFD, but retain configuration settings, use the command bfd disable. Public. Syntax: bfd.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the bfd command?
Syntax: bfd
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command enables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. BFD is asynchronous and echo mode is supported. The no form of this command disables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. Specifies the address on which to enable BFD in IPv4 format (x. x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. <IPv4-ADDR>. Syntax: bfd <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command?
Syntax: bfd <IPv4-ADDR>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the bfd all-interfaces command do?

The bfd all-interfaces command enables BFD on all OSPFv2 or OSPFv3 interfaces. The no form of this command disables BFD on all active OSPFv2/OSPFv3 or IPv4/IPv6 interfaces, excluding those on which BFD was enabled at the interface level with the commands ip ospf bfd and IPv6 o spfv3 bfd. Syntax: bfd all-interfaces.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the bfd all-interfaces command?
Syntax: bfd all-interfaces
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the bfd detect-multiplier command do?

The bfd detect-multiplier command sets BFD detection multiplier on an interface. The no form of this command removes the configured BFD detection multiplier. Public bfd detect-multiplier 43 Specifies the BFD detection multiplier. Range: 1 to 5. Default: 5. <MULTIPLIER>. Syntax: bfd detect-multiplier <MULTIPLIER>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the bfd detect-multiplier command?
Syntax: bfd detect-multiplier <MULTIPLIER>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the bfd disable command do?
The bfd disable command disables BFD on the switch, but retains all configuration settings. Syntax: bfd disable.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the bfd disable command?
Syntax: bfd disable
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the bfd enable command do?
The bfd enable command enables or disables BFD for HSC feature. Syntax: bfd enable.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the bfd enable command?
Syntax: bfd enable
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the bfd echo disable command do?

The bfd echo disable command disables support for BFD echo packets. Echo packet support is enabled by default. The no form of this command enables support for BFD echo packets. NOTE Toggling this feature on 8100, 8325, 8360, 9300, 9300S, 10040 switches may cause route flapping. NOTE BFD IPv6 Echo is not supported. Syntax: bfd echo disable.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the bfd echo disable command?
Syntax: bfd echo disable
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the bfd echo-src-ip-address command do?

The bfd echo-src-ip-address command sets the source IPv4 address for BFD echo packets. This address is used in all echo sessions. NOTE The source IP address must not be on the same network segment as any switch interface, otherwise a large number of ICMP redirect packets may be sent by the remote device, causing network congestion. The no form of this command removes the source IPv4 address for BFD echo packets, which causes the switch to stop sending echo packets. When a valid value is set, all sessions with a peer that is capable of receiving echo packets, will start transmitting echo packets. BFD control sessions continue to run concurrently with echo packets. Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255. <IPv4-ADDR>. Syntax: bfd echo-src-ip-address <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the bfd echo-src-ip-address command?
Syntax: bfd echo-src-ip-address <IPv4-ADDR>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the bfd min-echo-receive-interval command do?

The bfd min-echo-receive-interval command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD echo packets. The no form of this command removes the configured BFD echo packets interval. If the interval is not set, the default interval is used. NOTE BFD IPv6 Echo is not supported. Specifies the minimum reception interval in milliseconds. A valu e of 0 means that the switch does not support reception of BFD echo packets. Range: 0, 50 to 1000. Default: 500. <INTERVAL>. Syntax: bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the bfd min-echo-receive-interval command?
Syntax: bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the bfd min-transmit-interval command do?

The bfd min-transmit-interval command sets the minimum time interval between transmitted BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command removes the configured BFD minimum transmitted interval on an interface. If the interval is not set, the default interval is used. <INTERVAL> Specifies the minimum transmit interval in milliseconds. Range: 500 to 20000 50 to 20000 (8320, 8325, 9300/9300S, 10040). Default: 3000. Public bfd min-transmit-interval 55. Syntax: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the bfd min-transmit-interval command?
Syntax: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear bfd statistics command do?

The clear bfd statistics command clears statistics for all BFD sessions or for a specific BFD session. Specifies a session ID. Public clear bfd statistics 57. Syntax: clear bfd statistics [session <ID>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the clear bfd statistics command?
Syntax: clear bfd statistics [session <ID>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf bfd command do?

The ip ospf bfd command enables BFD for OSPFv2 on the current interface. The interface must have OSPFv2 enabled on it. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. The no form of this command sets the current interface to the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: ip ospf bfd.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip ospf bfd command?
Syntax: ip ospf bfd
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf bfd disable command do?
The ip ospf bfd disable command disables BFD for OSPFv2 on the current interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: ip ospf bfd disable.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip ospf bfd disable command?
Syntax: ip ospf bfd disable
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command enables BFD for OSPFv3 on the current interface. The interface must have OSPFv3 enabled on it. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. The no form of this command sets the current interface to the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Public IPv6 ospfv3 bfd 63. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ospfv3-af IPv4 bfd command do?

The ospfv3-af IPv4 bfd command enables BFD for OSPFv3 AF on the current interface. The interface must have OSPFv3 AF enabled on it. Public ospfv3-af IPv4 bfd 68 The no form of this command disables BFD on the current interface. NOTE OSPFv3 AF IPv4 sessions are established through the IPv6 link-local address. Therefore, a BFD session is also created for the IPv6 link-local address to monitor the path and establish neighborship. Syntax: ospfv3-af IPv4 bfd.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ospfv3-af IPv4 bfd command?
Syntax: ospfv3-af IPv4 bfd
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ospfv3-af IPv6 bfd command do?

The ospfv3-af IPv6 bfd command enables BFD for OSPFv3 AF on the current interface. The interface must have OSPFv3 AF enabled on it. The no form of this command disables BFD on the current interface. Syntax: ospfv3-af IPv6 bfd.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ospfv3-af IPv6 bfd command?
Syntax: ospfv3-af IPv6 bfd
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about ERPS Commands?

ERPS Commands: Limitations, Conflicts, or Exclusions • ERPS coexists with STP with the following limitations: Public Limitations, Conflicts, or Exclusions 82 ◦ ERPS control VLANs shall not be part of RPVST. ◦ Ring ports are excluded from STP operation. NOTE It is not recommended to configure any Spanning Tree interface context- related commands on the ERPS ring port. Before configuring ring port ensure all Spanning Tree interface context-related commands are removed from the interface. ◦ With VSX, STP operates on ISL ports despite being ring ports. ◦ Only default MSTP instance (CIST) is supported with ERPS. ◦ ERPS cannot be enabled with more than 252 RPVST instances. This limitation is applicable for all supported platforms. • Dynamic VLANs (MVRP) are not supported on ERPS ring ports. • MCLAG configuration on ERPS ring ports is not supported. • ERPS and loop protect are not supported on the same port. If enabled together, the behavior is undefined. • Active GW is not recommended on VLANs that are not part of VSX-LAGs.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> command do?

The clear erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> command removes the protection switching and triggers reversion both in revertive and non-revertive operation. This command will not change the configured revertive operation mode. Public clear erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> 85 Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239. <RINGID> <ID>. Syntax: clear erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the clear erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> command?
Syntax: clear erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring command do?

The erps ring command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID>. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the erps ring command?
Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> <port0 port1> interface command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> <port0 port1> interface command this command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <PORT0> Required, set port0 of the ring. Required, set port1 of the ring. Public erps ring <RINGID> <port0|port1> interface 90 Required, interface name (string). Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.

Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> description command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> description command this command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. The no form of this command removes the ring instance description. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <LINE>. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the erps ring <RINGID> description command?
Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> guard-interval 93 command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> guard-interval 93 command guard timer is used in nodes recovering from a local failure to avoid loops due to earlier Signal Fail (SF) messages that may be in the ring. The configuration specifies the guard timer duration in units of 10 ms. The timer period must be greater than the maximum expected forwarding delay in which an R-APS message traverses the entire ring. The default value is 50. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the guard interval and sets it to the default value of 50. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 Required, specifies the guard timer duration in units of 10 ms. Default: 50. <RINGID> <10 milliseconds>. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the erps ring <RINGID> guard-interval 93 command?
Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> instance command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> instance command on a common ERPS network, a physical ring can be configured with a single ERPS ring, and only one blocked port can be specified in the ring. When the ERPS ring is in normal state, the blocked port prohibits all service packets from passing through. As a result, all service data is transmitted through one path over the ERPS ring, and the other link on the blocked port becomes idle, leading to ineffective use of bandwidth. To improve link use efficiency, logical rings can be configured in the same physical ring in the ERPS multi-instance. A port may have different roles in different ERPS rings and different ERPS rings use different control VLANs. An ERPS ring must be configured with an ERP instance, and each ERP instance specifies a range of VLANs. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the erps ring <RINGID> instance command?
Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> description command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> description command this command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring instance. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. The no form of this command removes the ring instance description. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <ID> <LINE>. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> description command?
Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> enable command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> enable command this configuration enables protection switching on the given instance of the ring. It is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables protection switching on the given instance of the ring. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <ID>. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> enable command?
Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> protected-vl command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> protected-vl command this command specifies the set of VLANs that are protected by this ring instance. The no form of this command removes a set of VLANs that are protected by this ring instance. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> Public erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> protected-vlans 104 Required, specifies the ERPS ring instance identifier. Range: 1 -2. <ID>. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> protected-vl command?
Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> role 110 command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> role 110 command in ERPS, there is a central node called RPL Owner Node which blocks one of the ports to ensure that there is no loop formed for the Ethernet traffic. The link blocked by the RPL owner node is called the Ring Protection Link or RPL. The node at the other end of the RPL is known as RPL Neighbor Node. It uses R-APS control messages to coordinate the activities of switching on/off the RPL link. This command specifies the role of the node as owner or neighbor. The no form of this command removes the configuration of the node role from the instance. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <ID> <RPL-OWNER> <RPL-NEIGHBOR> Required, specifies the ERPS ring instance identifier. Range: 1 -2. Blocks traffic at one end of the RPL. The blocked end sends out periodic R-APS. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> role 110 command?
Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> rpl command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> rpl command in ERPS, there is a central node called RPL Owner Node which blocks one of the ports to ensure that there is no loop formed for the Ethernet traffic. The link blocked by the RPL owner node is called the Ring Protection Link or RPL. The node at the other end of the RPL is known as RPL Neighbor Node. It uses R-APS control messages to coordinate the activities of switching the RPL link on and off. This command specifies which of the ERPS ring ports is the RPL. The no form of this command removes the RPL port configuration from the ERPS ring instance. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <ID> <PORT0> <PORT1>. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> rpl command?
Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erps ring <RINGID> parent-ring command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> parent-ring command this command associates a sub-ring to a parent-ring and is required for the sub-ring to notify the parent- ring on change in topology. The no form of this command removes the parent ring identifier. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <RINGID>. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the erps ring <RINGID> parent-ring command?
Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf area command do?

The ip ospf area command runs the OSPF protocol on the interface with the configured IPv4 address for the area specified. The interfaces which have an IP address configured in this network or in a subset of this network, will participate in the OSPF protocol. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol. Specifies the OSPF process Id. Range: 1 to 65535. <PROCESS-ID> Public ip ospf area 84 <AREA-ID> Specifies the OSPF area ID in one of the following formats. Syntax: ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip ospf area command?
Syntax: ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf authentication-key command do?

The ip ospf authentication-key command sets the authentication password used for simple-text authentication. If the password is given in ciphertext it will be decrypted and applied to the protocol. The no form of this command deletes the authentication password used for simple-text authentication. {ciphertext | plaintext} Selects the password format. Specifies the password. Public ip ospf authentication-key 87 NOTE When the password is not provided on the command line, plaintext password prompting occurs upon pressing Enter. The entered password characters are masked with asterisks. Syntax: ip ospf authentication-key [{ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip ospf authentication-key command?
Syntax: ip ospf authentication-key [{ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf cost command do?

The ip ospf cost command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface. The interface cost is used as a parameter to calculate the best routes. The no form of this command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface to the default cost 1. Specifies the interface cost value. Range: 1 to 65535. Default: 1. <INTERFACE-COST>. Syntax: ip ospf cost <INTERFACE-COST>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip ospf cost command?
Syntax: ip ospf cost <INTERFACE-COST>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf dead-interval command do?

The ip ospf dead-interval command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead if no hello packet is received on the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead, to the default for the OSPF interface. The default value is 40 seconds (generally 4 times the hello packet interval). Specifies the time interval for the dead interval, in seconds. Ran ge: 1 to 65535. Default: 40. <INTERVAL>. Syntax: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip ospf dead-interval command?
Syntax: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf hello-interval command do?

The ip ospf hello-interval command sets the time interval between OSPF hello packets for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the time interval OSPF hello packets to the default of 10 seconds for the OSPF interface. Specifies the time interval for the hello interval, in seconds. Ran ge: 1 to 65535. Default: 10. <INTERVAL> Public ip ospf hello-interval 91. Syntax: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip ospf hello-interval command?
Syntax: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf keychain command do?

The ip ospf keychain command sets the key chain for md5 authentication. A key chain configures rotating keys for packet authenticating, reducing the risk of keys being compromised. The no form of this command deletes the key chain used for md5 authentication. Public ip ospf keychain 92 Name of key chain to be used for md5 authentication. <KEYCHAIN-NAME>. Syntax: ip ospf keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>.

Syntax: ip ospf keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>

The ip ospf message-digest-key md5 command sets the md5 message digest authentication key. If the md5 key is given in ciphertext, it will be decrypted and applied to the protocol. The no form of this command deletes the md5 authentication key. Specifies the md5 key ID. Range: 1 to 255. <KEY-ID> {ciphertext | plaintext} Selects the md5 key format. Specifies the md5 authentication key. <KEY> NOTE When the authentication key is not provided on the command line, plaintext key prompting occurs upon pressing Enter. The entered key characters are masked with asterisks. Syntax: ip ospf message-digest-key <KEY-ID> md5 [{ciphertext | plaintext} <KEY>].

Syntax: ip ospf message-digest-key <KEY-ID> md5 [{ciphertext | plaintext} <KEY>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf priority command do?

The ip ospf priority command sets the OSPF priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chances for it to become the designated router. Setting a priority of zero makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPF priority for the interface to the default of 1. Specifies the OSPF priority value. Range: 0 to 255. Default: 1. <PRIORITY-VALUE>. Syntax: ip ospf priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf retransmit-interval command do?

The ip ospf retransmit-interval command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default of 5 seconds for the OSPF interface. Specifies the retransmit interval, in seconds. Range: 1 to 3600. Default: 5. Public ip ospf retransmit-interval 99. Syntax: ip ospf retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip ospf retransmit-interval command?
Syntax: ip ospf retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf sha-key sha command do?

The ip ospf sha-key sha command sets the SHA (secure hash authentication) key for the selected interface. If the SHA key is given in ciphertext, it will be decrypted and applied to the protocol. This command accepts a key of up to 64 characters irrespective of the SHA version configured on the interface. OSPF will internally pad zeros to the key to obtain a 64-byte key. For all types of SHA, key length is adjusted to 64 bytes. The no form of this command deletes the SHA authentication key. Specifies the SHA key ID. Range: 1 to 255. <KEY-ID> {ciphertext | plaintext} Selects the SHA key format. Specifies the SHA authentication key. <KEY> NOTE When the authentication key is not provided on the command line, plaintext key prompting occurs upon pressing Enter. The entered key characters are masked with asterisks. Syntax: ip ospf sha-key <KEY-ID> sha [{ciphertext | plaintext} <KEY>].

Syntax: ip ospf sha-key <KEY-ID> sha [{ciphertext | plaintext} <KEY>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf shutdown command do?

The ip ospf shutdown command disables OSPF on the interface. The interface state changes to Down. It does not remove the interface from the OSPF area. To remove the interface, use the command no ip ospf area. The no form of this command re-enables OSPF on the interface. Syntax: ip ospf shutdown.

Syntax: ip ospf shutdown
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip ospf transit-delay command do?

The ip ospf transit-delay command sets the time delay in link state transmission for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the delay in link state transmission to the default of 1 second for the OSPF interface. Specifies the transit delay in seconds. Range: 1 to 3600. Default: 1. <DELAY>. Syntax: ip ospf transit-delay <DELAY>.

Syntax: ip ospf transit-delay <DELAY>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the neighbor command do?

The neighbor command this command configures a static OSPFv2 neighbor for point-to-multipoint networks. Static neighbors are required for NBMA (Non-Broadcast Multiple Access) networks, where automatic neighbor discovery through multicast hello packets is not possible. The neighbor IP address should be the IP address of the interface on the neighboring router within the same network segment. The no form of this command removes a previously configured static OSPFv2 neighbor from the point-to- multipoint network configuration. neighbor <neighbor-ip> Static neighbor IP address for point-to-multipoint networks. Syntax: neighbor <neighbor-ip>.

Syntax: neighbor <neighbor-ip>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the router-id command do?

The router-id command sets an ID for the router in an IPv4 address format. The no form of this command unconfigures the router-id for the instance and sets the router-id to the default as follows: the router-id is selected dynamically as equal to the highest loopback address on the router, or the highest active interface if there are no loopback addresses. If no IP address is configured on any interfaces on the router, OSPF will not form an adjacency. Specifies the Router ID in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255. <ROUTER-ADDR>. Syntax: router-id <ROUTER-ADDR>.

Syntax: router-id <ROUTER-ADDR>

The show ip ospf interface command displays general OSPF, area, state, and configuration information. NOTE For an IP unnumbered enabled OSPF interface, the output of the command displays the IP address borrowed from the donor interface. Enter an OSPF process ID to display general OSPF information for a particular OSPF process. Range: 1 to 65535. <PROCESS-ID> <interface-name>] brief Specify the name of an OSPF interface. Include this parameter to display a brief overview of the followi ng OSPF configuration information. Public show ip ospf interface 121 Interface: OSPF interface name. • • Area: OSPF area ID. • Cost: The metric OSPF uses to judge a path's feasibility, calc ulated as (reference bandwidth / interface bandwidth). Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<interface-name>] [brief].

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show ip ospf lsdb command do?

The show ip ospf lsdb command shows the OSPF link state database summary for different OSPF LSAs (Link State Advertisement). Use the parameters to get information for a particular LSA. Enter an OSPF process ID to display general OSPF information for a particular OSPF process. Range: 1 to 65535. <PROCESS-ID> adv-router {<ROUTER-ID>| self} Select to display link states for a particular advertising router. S pecify either a Router ID of the advertising router or specify sel f to show self-originated link states. area <AREA-ID> Select to display information filtered for the specified area in on e of the following formats. • OSPF area identifier in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a d ecimal number from 0 to 255. • OSPF area identifier in decimal format. Value: 0 to 4294967 295. Syntax: show ip ospf [<process-id>] lsdb.

Syntax: show ip ospf [<process-id>] lsdb
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show ip ospf neighbors command do?

The show ip ospf neighbors command displays information about OSPF neighbors. <PROCESS-ID> Enter an OSPF process ID to display OSPF neighbor informatio n for the particular OSPF process. Range: 1 to 65535. neighbors <NEIGHBOR-ID> Select to display information about a particular neighbor, specifi ed in IPv4 format (A. B. C. D). interface <INTERFACE-NAME> Select to display neighbor information only for the specified in terface. detail summary all-vrfs Select to display detailed information for all the neighbors. Select to display summary information for the neighbors. Select to display neighbor information for all VRFs. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specify the name of a VRF. Default: default. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>].

Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show ip ospf routes command do?

The show ip ospf routes command displays OSPF routing information. <IPv4-ADDR> Specify an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a deci mal number from 0 to 255. Public show ip ospf routes 133 Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 32. <MASK> <PROCESS-ID> all-vrfs vrf <VRF-NAME> protected Enter an OSPF process ID to display OSPF neighbor informatio n for the particular OSPF process. Range: 1 to 65535. Select to display neighbor information for all VRFs. Specify the name of a VRF. Default: default. Selects a protected route. For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command do?

The clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command clears the OSPFv3 event statistics. <PROCESS-ID> <INTERFACE-NAME> Specifies the OSPFv3 process ID to clear the statistics for the p articular OSPFv3 process. Range: 1 to 65535. Specifies the OSPFv3 statistics to clear for the specified interfa ce. all-vrfs Select to clear the OSPFv3 statistics for all VRFs. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of a VRF. Configures OSPFv3 to advertise the default route (: : /0) to its neighbors if it is present in the routing table. Optionally, the metric value can be set for default route: : /0. The default value is 1. The no form of this command disables advertisement of the default route. metric <METRIC-VALUE> Specifies the OSPF metric value for the default route. Optional. Default: 1. Syntax: clear IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]].

Syntax: clear IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]]

The IPv6 ospfv3 area command runs the OSPFv3 protocol on the interface for the area specified. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol Public IPv6 ospfv3 area 191 Specifies the area ID is one of the following formats. • Area ID in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal num ber from 0 to 255. • Area ID as a decimal value. For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command configures IPSec AH authentication. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec. The no form of this command removes IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> Specifies the Security Parameters Index (SPI) to use. The SPI is an identification tag carried in the IPsec AH header. It enables t he receiving OSPF process to select and use the Security Association (SA) from the SA table. The SPI must be unique on the sw itch. Range: 256 to 4294967295. Specifies the authentication type: md5 or sha1. Specifies the key type to use: plaintext (unencrypted), hex-st ring (encrypted) or ciphertext (encrypted). Specifies the authentication key. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX>.

The IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command sets the interval after a neighbor is declared dead when no hello packet is received on the OSPFv3 interface. Public IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval 196 The no form of this command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead, to the default for the OSPFv3 interface. The default value is 40 seconds (generally four times the hello packet interval). Specifies the time interval for the dead interval, in seconds. Ran ge: 1 to 65535. Default: 40. <INTERVAL>. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval <INTERVAL>.

The IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command configures NULL ESP on an interface which disables IPsec ESP. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command sets the time interval between OSPFv3 hello packets for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the time interval between OSPFv3 hello packets to the default for the OSPFv3 interface of 10 seconds. Specifies the time interval between hello packets, in seconds. For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 passive command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 passive command configures the interface as an OSPFv3 passive interface. With this setting, the interface participates in the OSPF, but does not send or receive OSPF packets on that interface. The no form of this command resets the interface as active. For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 priority command sets the OSPFv3 priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chance it will become the designated router. Setting a priority of 0 makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPFv3 priority for the interface to the default of 1. Specifies the OSPFv3 priority value. Default: 1. Range: 0 to 255. <number-value>. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 priority <number-value>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 priority <number-value>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command disables OSPFv3 on the interface. The interface state changes to Down. It does not remove the interface from the OSPF area. To remove the interface, use the command no ip ospf area. The no form of this command re-enables OSPFv3 on the interface. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command sets the time delay in Link state transmission for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the transit delay in Link state transmission to the default of 1 second. Specifies the time delay for the transit delay, in seconds. Range: 1 to 3600. Default: 1. <DELAY>. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay <DELAY>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the router ospfv3 command do?

The router ospfv3 command creates the OSPFv3 process (if not created already) and enters the router OSPFv3 instance context. Optionally if specified, you can specify a named VRF, or the default VRF if the <vrf-name> is not specified. Only one OSPFv3 process is allowed per VRF. The no form of this command removes the OSPFv3 instance. If a VRF is specified, it removes the OSPF instance from the named VRF, or the default VRF if the <var-name> is not specified. Specifies an OSPFv3 process ID. Length: 1 to 65535. <PROCESS-ID> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Public router ospfv3 218. Syntax: router ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the router ospfv3 command?
Syntax: router ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command do?

The show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command shows the OSPFv3 routing table entries for Area Border Router (ABR) and Autonomous System Border Router (ASBR). Specifies an OSPFv3 process ID to show the OSPFv3 routing ta ble entries for ABR and ASBR for the particular OSPFv3 proces s. Range: 1 to 65535. Select to show OSPFv3 border router information for all VRFs. Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. <PROCESS-ID> all-vrfs vrf <VRF-NAME>. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] border-routers [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] border-routers [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command do?

The show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command shows information about OSPFv3 enabled interfaces. <PROCESS-ID> <INTERFACE-NAME> brief Specifies an OSPFv3 process ID optionally to show the OSPFv3 enabled interfaces for the particular OSPFv3 process. Range: 1 to 65535. Selects to show information only for the specified OSPFv3-en abled interface. Include this parameter to display a brief overview of the followi ng OSPF configuration information. Interface: OSPF interface name. • • Area: OSPF area ID. • Cost: The metric OSPF uses to judge a path's feasibility, calc ulated as (reference bandwidth / interface bandwidth). • State: Indicates if the interface is a designated router (Dr) o r a backup designated router (Backup-dr). • Status: Indicates if the interface is up or down. • Flags: P - Passive A - Active. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [brief].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command?

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 lsdb command do?

The show IPv6 ospfv3 lsdb command shows the OSPFv3 link state database summary for different OSPF LSAs (Link State Advertisement). Use the parameters to get information for a particular LSA. Enter an OSPFv3 process ID to display general OSPF informatio n for a particular OSPF process. Range: 1 to 65535. <PROCESS-ID> adv-router {<ROUTER-ID>| self} Select to display link states for a particular advertising router. S pecify either a Router ID of the advertising router or specify sel f to show self-originated link states. area <AREA-ID> Select to display information filtered for the specified area in on e of the following formats. • OSPFv3 area identifier in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. • OSPFv3 area identifier in decimal format. Value: 0 to 42949 67295. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<process-id>] lsdb.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 lsdb command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<process-id>] lsdb

The show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command shows information about OSPFv3 neighbors. <PROCESS-ID> Specifies an OSPFv3 process ID to show OSPFv3 neighbor infor mation for the particular OSPFv3 process. Range: 1 to 65535. neighbors <NEIGHBOR-ID> Shows information about a particular neighbor, specified in IP v 4 format (A. B. C. D). interface <INTERFACE-NAME> Shows neighbor information only for the specified interface. detail summary all-vrfs Shows detailed information for the neighbors. Shows summary information for the neighbors. Shows neighbor information for all VRFs. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>].

Select to show OSPFv3 routing table information for all VRFs. Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>].

The show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command shows the OSPFv3 statistics for the OSPFv3-enabled interfaces. <PROCESS-ID> <INTERFACE-NAME> all-vrfs Specifies an OSPFv3 process ID to show OSPF-enabled inter face statistics information on the specified OSPFv3 process. Ra nge: 1 to 65535. Selects to show information only for the specified interface. Select to show OSPF-enabled interface statistics information for all VRFs. Public show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface 237 Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Summarizes the external routes with the matching address and mask. When advertising this route, its metric is set to the lowest cost path from among the routes that were summarized. Public summary-address 239 The no form of this command disables route summarization. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>].

The diag ospfv3 lsdb dump command this command dumps the OSPFv3 AF protocol LSA information. Disables the OSPFv3 AF process. By default OSPFv3 AF process is enabled. This command does not remove the OSPFv3 AF configurations. Public disable 307. Syntax: diag ospfv3 lsdb dump.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the router ospfv3-af command do?

The router ospfv3-af command creates the OSPFv3 AF process (if not created already) and enters the router OSPFv3 AF instance context. Optionally if specified, you can specify a named VRF, or the default VRF if the <vrf-name> is not specified. Maximum of eight OSPFv3 processes are allowed per VRF. The switch allows configuration of OSPFv2, OSPFv3, OSPFv3 IPv4 AF and OSPFv3 IPv6 AF. But at any point only 8 OSPF instances can run per address-family, 8 instances for IPv4 (OSPFv2 + OSPFv3 IPv4 AF) and 8 instances for IPv6 (OSPFv3 + OSPFv3 IPv6 AF). The no form of this command removes the OSPFv3 AF instance. If a VRF is specified, it removes the OSPF instance from the named VRF, or the default VRF if the <var-name> is not specified. Public router ospfv3-af 367 Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID. Length: 1 to 65535. Syntax: router ospfv3-af <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

Syntax: router ospfv3-af <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show ip ecmp command do?

The show ip ecmp command displays the Equal Cost Multipath (ECMP) configuration. Public Overview 466 Configures a primary or secondary IPv4 or IPv6 address for the VRRP group. To use secondary IP addresses in a VRRP group, you must first configure a primary IP address on the same group. A maximum of 16 IP addresses per IPv4 VRRP group and 8 IPv6 addresses per IPv6 VRRP group are supported. NOTE Do not configure an IPv4 VRRP group using addresses from the /30, /31, and /32 subnets of the interface IP address. 16 Virtual IP addresses per IPv4 VR and 8 Virtual IP addresses per IPv6 VR are supported. NOTE The total number of VIP s supported by a switch is: • 1024 VIP s for IPv4 VRs • 512 VIP s for IPv6 VRs The no form of this command deletes a primary or secondary IPv4 or IPv6 address from the VRRP group. Syntax: show ip ecmp.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the router vrrp {enable disable} command do?

The router vrrp {enable disable} command enables or disables VRRP protocol globally. You must globally enable the VRRP feature for VRRP virtual router. no router vrrp enable disables VRRP protocol globally but does not remove all VRRP configurations. no router vrrp disable enables VRRP protocol globally. Syntax: router vrrp {enable | disable}.

The no router vrrp command removes VRRP configuration and VRRP global protocol. If auto-confirm is enabled or VRRP is not configured on any interface, this command will not ask for user confirmation. Syntax: no router vrrp.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the apply policy command do?

The apply policy command applies a classifier policy containing a PBR action to an interface. A policy with PBR actions is only applicable to L3/routing interfaces. The no form of this command removes a classifier policy containing a PBR action from an interface. config-if Specifies name of the policy. • Layer 3 LAGs are not supported on 5420 or 6200 series switches. PBR policies cannot be applied to Layer 3 LAGs. Syntax: apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the router rip command do?

The router rip command creates RIP process if not already created and enters the router rip <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. If no VRF is mentioned, a default is used. Only one RIP process is allowed per VRF. The no form of this command deletes the RIP instance for the VRF. If no VRF is mentioned the default is deleted. Specifies name of the RIP process ID. Range: 1-63. <PROCESS-ID> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies VRF name. Syntax: router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear ip rip statistics command do?

The clear ip rip statistics command clears RIP event statistics. Specifies RIP process ID. Range: 1-63 Clears statistics for all VRFs. Selects VRF to clear statistics for. Specifies VRF name. <PROCESS-ID> all-vrfs vrf <VRF-NAME> Enables RIP process for RIP enabled IP address configured on interface. The no form of this command disables RIP process on interface. Disables RIP process for RIP enabled IP addresses configured on interface. The no form of this command enables RIP process on interface. Disables an interface from sending RIP packets for a specific IP address. The no form of this command enables interface for sending RIP packets for a specific IP address. Disables interface from receiving RIP packets for a specific IP address. Syntax: clear ip rip [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].

The clear IPv6 ripng statistics command clears RIPng event statistics. Public RIPng clear commands 618 Specifies RIPng process ID. Range: 1-63 <PROCESS-ID> all-vrfs vrf <VRF-NAME> Clears statistics for all VRFs. Selects VRF to clear statistics for. Specifies VRF name. Enables RIPng process on interface. The no form of this command disables RIPng process on interface. Disables RIPng process on interface. The no form of this command enables RIPng process on interface. Disables interface from sending RIPng packets. An interface can send RIPng packets by default. The no form of this command enables interface to send RIPng packets, if disabled. Disables interface from receiving RIPng packets for all enabled IP addresses. An interface can receive RIPng packets by default. Syntax: clear IPv6 ripng [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the clear IPv6 ripng statistics command?
Syntax: clear IPv6 ripng [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show capacities ripng command do?
The show capacities ripng command displays the maximum number of RIPng interfaces, routes and process. Syntax: show capacities ripng.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show capacities ripng command?
Syntax: show capacities ripng
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show capacities-status ripng command do?
The show capacities-status ripng command displays number of RIPng interfaces, routes and process configured in the system. Syntax: show capacities-status ripng.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, how do you configure IRDP?

To configure IRDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable IRDP on an interface with the command ip irdp 2. Set the maximum hold time with the command ip irdp holdtime 3. Set the maximum router advertisement interval with the command ip irdp maxadvertinterv 4. Set the minimum router advertisement interval with the command ip irdp minadvertinterv 5. Set the IRDP preference level with the command ip irdp preference 6. Review IRDP configuration settings with the command show ip irdp. Commands shown in the procedure include: interface vlan 2.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, how do you configure IPv6 RA?

To configure IPv6 RA, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable transmission of IPv6 router advertisements with the command no IPv6 nd suppress-r 2. Optionally, configure IPv6 unicast address prefixes with the command IPv6 nd prefix 3. Optionally, configure support for DNS name resolution with the commands IPv6 nd ra dns ser.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp snooping (config mode) command do?

The ip igmp snooping (config mode) command configures drop-unknown and fastlearn modes on the ports. While IGMP snooping is enabled, the traffic will be forwarded only to ports that made an IGMP request for the multicast. Drop unknown filters ensure that packets are not forwarded to ports that did not make a request for the traffic stream. This could either be a filter across all VLANs (vlan-shared) or per VLAN (vlan-exclusive). The default is vlan-shared. Fast learn enables the port to learn group information when receiving a topology change notification. By default, fast learn is not enabled on ports. Drop unknown filters ensure that packets are not forwarded to ports that did not make a request for the traffic stream. Public IGMP snooping commands 30 Enables a shared VLAN filter on the switch. Default is vlan-sh ared. Syntax: ip igmp snooping.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping (config mode) command?
Syntax: ip igmp snooping
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp snooping (vlan mode) command do?

The ip igmp snooping (vlan mode) command these commands enable or disable IP IGMP snooping on the VLAN, create IGMP snooping static multicast groups, set the IGMP snooping version and Configure the ACL on a particular interface. NOTE Disabling and enabling IGMP snooping on a VLAN causes IGMP querier re- election. Associates an ACL with the IGMP. Syntax: ip igmp snooping.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping (vlan mode) command?
Syntax: ip igmp snooping

section: ip igmp snooping apply access list

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp snooping apply access list command do?

The ip igmp snooping apply access list command configures the access list (ACL) in a particular interface to filter IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in a particular access list name. The no form of this command removes the configuration. Specifies the access list name. <ACL-NAME>. Syntax: ip igmp snooping apply access list <ACL-NAME>.

command: ip igmp snooping apply access list
syntax: ip igmp snooping apply access list <ACL-NAME>

section: ip igmp snooping apply access list

Syntax: ip igmp snooping apply access list <ACL-NAME>

syntax: ip igmp snooping apply access list <ACL-NAME>

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about Interface counters polling?

Interface counters polling: • Agent IP configuration for IPv4 and IPv6 • Header size configuration • Max datagram size configuration • Ingress sampling for all forwarded traffic (L2, L3) • Enable/Disable sFlow per interface • Support for three remote collectors • An out-of-band collector can be defined on the management VRF • A collector can be defined on the default and non-default VRF Public sFlow agent 52 • Sampling on Ethernet and LAG interfaces • High availability support (sampling continues to work after switch-over) • Source IP support (setting source IP for sFlow datagrams sent to a remote collector) NOTE For the 4100i, 6000, and 6100 switch series, collector can be defined only on the default VRF. Also, there is no sampling of egress traffic. Limitations • Sampling rate cannot be set per interface (global only) • sFlow is not configurable through SNMP.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear sflow statistics command do?

The clear sflow statistics command this command clears the sFlow sample statistics counter to 0 either globally or for a specific interface. Specifies all interfaces on the switch. interface <INTERFACE-NAME> Specifies the name of an interface on the switch. Syntax: clear sflow statistics {global | interface <INTERFACE-NAME>}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the clear sflow statistics command?
Syntax: clear sflow statistics {global | interface <INTERFACE-NAME>}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the sflow disable command do?
The sflow disable command disables the sFlow agent, but retains any existing sFlow configuration settings. The settings become active if the sFlow agent is re-enabled. Syntax: sflow disable.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the sflow disable command?
Syntax: sflow disable
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 dhcp command do?

The IPv6 dhcp command configure to enable DHCPv6 mode on a VLAN. In DHCPv6 Mode, the VLAN will get a DHCP-Server allocated IPv6 address along with DNS and NTP attributes. Public IPv6 dhcp 79 The no form of the command disables DHCP mode and is supported only on interface VLANs. NOTE ZTPv6 attributes are included in the request packet, but they are not utilized by ZTP on the data port. Currently, ZTPv6 attributes are only supported on the management port. Also, IPv6 addresses assigned through DHCPv6 only support a /64 prefix. Syntax: IPv6 dhcp.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the IPv6 dhcp command?
Syntax: IPv6 dhcp
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dhcp option command do?

The ip dhcp option command this command enables the DHCP client host name and broadcast flag globally. If the ip dhcp option broadcast-flag command is enabled, then the DHCP offer and ack packets in the DHCP requests will be treated as broadcast packets. These packets will not be forwarded due to the presence of a default static route. The no form of this command globally disables the host name and DHCP client broadcast flag options. NOTE The ip dhcp option broadcast-flag command should be configured before configuring the ip dhcp command. Syntax: ip dhcp option [host-name | broadcast-flag].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip dhcp option command?
Syntax: ip dhcp option [host-name | broadcast-flag]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the no ip dhcp preferred-vlan vlan <VLAN-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The no ip dhcp preferred-vlan vlan <VLAN-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command specifies the preferred VLAN for DHCP client options, including DNS, NTP, and Default Gateway. The preferred VLAN is the VLAN used to select DHCP client options within a specified VRF when multiple VLANs have DHCP client enabled in the same VRF. If no VRF is specified, the configuration applies to the default VRF. In cases where the preferred VLAN is not explicitly configured, the lowest VLAN with DHCP client enabled will be automatically selected. The no form of the command removes the preferred VLAN configuration and restores the default behavior. VLAN ID to set as preferred VLAN. VLAN identifier range: 1 to 4094. Specifies the VRF in which the preferred VLANshould be confi gured.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the dhcp-relay hop-count-increment command do?

The dhcp-relay hop-count-increment command enables the DHCP relay hop count increment feature, which causes the DHCP relay agent to increment the hop count in all relayed DHCP packets. Hop count is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables the hop count increment feature. Syntax: dhcp-relay hop-count-increment.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcp-relay hop-count-increment command?
Syntax: dhcp-relay hop-count-increment

section: 4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address?

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about 4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address? ?

4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address? : The factory default VLAN to which the DHCP address is assigned is VLAN1. Users can configure the VLAN for DHCP allocation on platforms supporting multiple VLAN configurations. Only a single VLAN can support DHCP assignments. Public FAQ 122 5. Can DHCP assignments be disabled in an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch? Use the no ip dhcp command on the required VLAN interface or management VRF port you can disable the DHCP assignments in an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch. A static configuration option is available on both ports. 6. Can IP DHCP switch assignment be carried out on a Routed only Port (RoP)? No, RoP is not supported. Only VLAN and OOBM ports can be allocated an address with DHCP. 7. Can an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch support DHCP IPv6 Allocation? Yes, an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch can support DHCP IPv6 allocation. However, for Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP), IPv4 is required. 8.
