For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear dhcp-snooping statistics command do?
The clear dhcp-snooping statistics command clears all DHCP snooping statistics. Syntax: clear dhcp-snooping statistics.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the clear dhcp-snooping statistics command?
Syntax: clear dhcp-snooping statistics
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding command do?

The dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding command allows binding to be overwritten for the same IP address. When enabled, and a DHCP server offers a host an IP address that is already bound to an existing host in the binding table, the existing binding is overwritten for the new host if the new host is successfully able to acquire the same IP address. This overwriting is disabled by default, causing the DHCP server offers to be dropped. The no form of the command disables DHCP snooping overwrite binding. Syntax: dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command adds an authorized (trusted) DHCP server to a list of authorized servers for use by DHCP snooping. This command can be issued multiple times, adding a maximum of 20 authorized servers per VRF. By default, Public dhcp-snooping authorized-server 142 with an empty list of authorized servers, all DHCP servers are considered to be trusted for DHCP snooping purposes. NOTE The mgmt VRF cannot be used with this command. The no form of this command deletes the specified DHCP server from the authorized list. Specifies the IP address of the trusted DHCP server. <IP-ADDR> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the VRF name. Syntax: dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the dhcp-snooping event-log client command do?

The dhcp-snooping event-log client command this command enables or disables dhcp-snooping client level event logs that help with client telemetry on a remote management station such as HPE Aruba Networking Central. By default, client level event logs are disabled. The no form of this command disables client-level event logs for DHCP snooping after they are enabled. View these logged DHCP snooping events by issuing the command show events -c dhcp-snooping. NOTE For additional information on DHCP-related event logging, please refer to the Event Log Message Reference Guide. Syntax: dhcp-snooping event-log client.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping event-log client command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping event-log client
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command do?

The dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command configures external storage to be used for backing up IP bindings (used by DHCP snooping) to a file. When configured, the switch stores all the IP bindings in an external storage file so that they are retained after the switch restarts. When the switch restarts, it reads the IP bindings from the configured external storage file to populate its local cache. NOTE When both external storage and flash storage are configured to store DHCP snooping IP bindings, the external storage takes priority, and is used exclusively until it becomes unconfigured, at which time flash storage (if configured) is used. Later, if external storage is configured again, flash storage stops and external storage resumes. The no form of this command disables the saving of IP bindings in an external storage file. Syntax: dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command do?

The dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command configures switch flash storage to be used for backing up client IP bindings (used by DHCP snooping). When flash storage is configured (and external storage is not already configured for this purpose), the switch stores the IP bindings in switch flash storage. When the switch restarts, it reads the IP bindings from the switch flash storage to populate its local cache. Writing the IP bindings to flash storage only occurs after the configured delay and if there has been a change in client IP bindings. Writing is skipped when client IP bindings have not changed since the previous write. Omitting delay <DELAY> sets the default delay of 900 seconds. Syntax: dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command do?

The dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command sets the maximum number of DHCP bindings allowed on the selected interface. For all interfaces on which this command is not run, the default max binding is the maximum value of the range. The no form of the command reverts max bindings for the selected interface to its default. Specifies the maximum number of DHCP bindings. Range: 1 to 256. <MAX-BINDINGS>. Syntax: dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the dhcp-snooping static-attributes command do?

The dhcp-snooping static-attributes command enables storage of static attributes provided to the DHCP client by DHCP server during DHCP packet exchange. Disabled by default. When enabled, the following attributes are stored in OVSDB along with the client IP binding entry: 1. Name server IP addresses: DNS server IP s provided by the DHCP server to the client. Maximum: 3 per client. 2. Default gateway IP address: Router IP addresses provided by DHCP server to the client. Maximum: 3 per client. 3. Server IP address: IP address of the DHCP server that leased the IP to the client. The no form of the command disables storing of client static attributes. After disabling, existing client static attributes will be flushed. Syntax: dhcp-snooping static-attributes.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping static-attributes command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping static-attributes
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the dhcp-snooping trust command do?

The dhcp-snooping trust command enables DHCP snooping trust on the selected port. Only server packets received on trusted ports are forwarded. All the ports are untrusted by default. The no form of the command disables DHCP snooping trust on the selected port. Syntax: dhcp-snooping trust.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping trust command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping trust
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the dhcp-snooping verify mac command do?

The dhcp-snooping verify mac command this command enables verification of the hardware address field in DHCP client packets. When enabled, the DHCP client hardware address field and the source MAC address must be the same for packets received on untrusted ports or else the packet is dropped. This DHCP snooping MAC verification is enabled by default. The no form of the command disables DHCP snooping MAC verification. Public dhcp-snooping verify mac 154. Syntax: dhcp-snooping verify mac.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping verify mac command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping verify mac
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics command do?
The clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics command clears all DHCPv6 snooping statistics. Public clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics 162. Syntax: clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics command?
Syntax: clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping command?
Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command adds an authorized (trusted) DHCPv6 server to a list of authorized servers for use by DHCPv6 snooping. This command can be issued multiple times, adding a maximum of 20 authorized servers per VRF. By Public dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server 166 default, with an empty list of authorized servers, all DHCPv6 servers are considered to be trusted for DHCPv6 snooping purposes. NOTE The mgmt VRF cannot be used with this command. NOTE Configure the link local IPv6 address instead of global IPv6 address of the DHCPv6 server as the authorized-server. For example: switch(config)# dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server fe80: : 2ca4: fa40: d4cd: bc2f The no form of this command deletes the specified DHCPv6 server from the authorized list. Specifies the IPv6 address of the trusted DHCPv6 server. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command?
Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]

The dhcpv6-snooping event-log client command this command enables or disables DHCPv6 snooping client level event logs that help with client telemetry on a remote management station such as HPE Aruba Networking Central. By default, client level event logs are disabled. The no form of this command disables client-level event logs for DHCPv6 snooping after they are enabled. View these logged DHCPv6 snooping events by issuing the command show events -c dhcpv6-snooping. NOTE For additional information on DHCP-related event logging, please refer to the Event Log Message Reference Guide. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping event-log client.

Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping event-log client

The dhcpv6-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command configures external storage to be used for backing up IPv6 bindings (used by DHCPv6 snooping) to a file. When configured, the switch stores all the IP bindings in an external storage file so that they are retained after the switch restarts. When the switch restarts, it reads the IPv6 bindings from the configured external storage file to populate its local cache. NOTE When both external storage and flash storage are configured to store DHCP snooping IP bindings, the external storage takes priority, and is used exclusively until it becomes unconfigured, at which time flash storage (if configured) is used. Later, if external storage is configured again, flash storage stops and external storage resumes. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command?
Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME>

The dhcpv6-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command configures switch flash storage to be used for backing up client IP bindings (used by DHCPv6 snooping). When flash storage is configured (and external storage is not already configured for this purpose), the switch stores the IP bindings in switch flash storage. Writing the IP bindings to flash storage only occurs after the configured delay and if there has been a change in client IP bindings. Writing is skipped when client IP bindings have not changed since the previous write. Omitting delay <DELAY> sets the default delay of 900 seconds. section: 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN?

5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN: a. Check if the server is reachable on the same VLAN as the client. b. If the server is on a different L3 network, configure DHCP-Relay to forward the packets between the client and server. 6. Authorized server configurations-When configured, the server assigning IP address should be one of the IP addresses from the authorized server list. In DHCP snooping IPv6, if DHCP Relay is between a test device and a server, the authorised server configuration should include a link to the local IP address of the relay interface. This is because, while forwarding the server's response to the client, it will stamp a link to the local IP address as the source IP of the packet. Public.

topic: 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the no IPv4 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no IPv4 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command adds static IPv4 client source binding information to the switch IP binding database. Although DHCPv4 snooping is often used to dynamically populate the binding database, this command is available for manually adding entries to the switch IP binding database. NOTE Statically configured IP binding information supersedes any dynamically collected binding information for the same client. The no form of this command removes the specified binding that was statically configured with the IPv4 source-binding command. The no form has no effect on bindings that were dynamically configured with DHCPv4 snooping. Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv4 source-lockdown command do?

The IPv4 source-lockdown command enables IPv4 source lockdown for all VLANs on the selected interface (port). The no form of this command disables IPv4 source lockdown for the selected interface (port). CAUTION This configuration will disable flow tracking statistics collection. Syntax: IPv4 source-lockdown.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the IPv4 source-lockdown command?
Syntax: IPv4 source-lockdown
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show IPv4 source-binding command do?
The show IPv4 source-binding command shows all IPv4 static source binding information irrespective of source lockdown configuration. Syntax: show IPv4 source-binding.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show IPv4 source-binding command?
Syntax: show IPv4 source-binding
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about VLAN Id: 2000?

VLAN Id: 2000: MAC address: 00: 50: 56: 96: e4: cf IP Address: 192.168.142.113 Time Remaining: static Lockdown Status: Yes Hardware Status: Yes Hardware Error Reason: -- Showing the detailed binding record and related information for interface 1/1/2 (identified in this example command by the VLAN): switch# show IPv4 source-lockdown binding vlan 100.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command adds static IPv6 client source binding information to the switch IPv6 binding database. Although DHCPv6 snooping is often used to dynamically populate the binding database, this command is available for manually adding entries to the switch IPv6 binding database. NOTE Statically configured IPv6 binding information supersedes any dynamically collected binding information for the same client. The no form of this command removes the specified binding that was statically configured with the IPv6 source-binding command. The no form has no effect on bindings that were dynamically configured with DHCPv6 snooping. Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 source-lockdown hardware retry <VLAN-ID> command do?

The IPv6 source-lockdown hardware retry <VLAN-ID> command retries the IPv6 source lockdown hardware programming for a client identified by VLAN and IPv6 address. Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show IPv6 source-binding command do?
The show IPv6 source-binding command shows all IPv6 static source binding information irrespective of source lockdown configuration. Syntax: show IPv6 source-binding.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 source-binding command?
Syntax: show IPv6 source-binding
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dns domain-name command do?

The ip dns domain-name command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name. Public ip dns domain-name 216 | Parameter | | Description | | | | Specifies the domain name to append to DNS requests. Length: 1 to 256 characters. <DOMAIN-NAME> | vrf <VRF-NAME> | | Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-name command?
Syntax: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip dns host command do?

The ip dns host command associates a static IP address with a hostname. The DNS client returns this IP address instead of querying a DNS server for an IP address for the hostname. Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. host <HOST-NAME> Specifies the name of a host. Length: 1 to 256 characters. <IP-ADDR> Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255, or IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexa decimal number from 0 to F. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: ip dns host <HOST-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip dns host command?
Syntax: ip dns host <HOST-NAME>

section: Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A?

Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A: L3-GW(config)# vlan 100 L3-GW(config-vlan-100)# exit L3-GW(config)# interface 1/1/25 L3-GW(config-if)# vlan trunk allowed 100 L3-GW(config-if)# no shutdown L3-GW(config-if)# exit L3-GW(config)# interface vlan 100 L3-GW(config-if-vlan)# ip address 192.168.1.1/24 Now the clients will be able to ping the gateway IP; but will not be able to communicate with each other due to the isolation enforced with private VLAN. As expected, the ping from client 1 to client 2 will fail in the above example, although the clients are in the same subnet.

topic: Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the arp inspection trust command do?

The arp inspection trust command configures the interface as a trusted. All interfaces are untrusted by default. The no form of this command returns the interface to the default state (untrusted). Syntax: arp inspection trust.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the arp inspection trust command?
Syntax: arp inspection trust
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command do?

The arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command specifies a permanent static neighbor entry in the ARP table (for IPv4 neighbors). The no form of this command deletes a permanent static neighbor entry from the ARP table. Public arp ip 236 Specifies the IP address of the neighbor or the virtual IP addres s of the cluster in IP format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal num ber from 0 to 255.. Range: 4096 to 131072. Default: 131072. Specifies the MAC address of the neighbor or the multicast M AC address in IANA format (xx: xx: xx: xx: xx: xx), where x is a h exadecimal number from 0 to F. Range: 4096 to 131072. Defaul t: 131072. Syntax: arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command?
Syntax: arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the arp process-grat-arp command do?

The arp process-grat-arp command enables the processing of gratuitous ARP packets on the individual port or group of L3 ports together. By default, the gratuitous ARP processing is enabled. When gratuitous ARP (GARP) processing is enabled, a switch that is advertising any changes in its MAC through the GARP will reflect in the neighbor table of the switch. However, the switch will not be able to learn the neighbor through the GARP. This configuration is applicable only on L3 interfaces such as ROPs, subinterfaces, and SVIs. The no form of this command disables the processing of gratuitous ARP packets. Syntax: arp process-grat-arp.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the arp process-grat-arp command?
Syntax: arp process-grat-arp
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear arp command do?

The clear arp command clears IPv4 and IPv6 neighbor entries from the ARP table. If you do not specify any VRF or port parameters, ARP table entries are cleared for the default VRF. Specifies a port on the switch. For example: vlan 2. ip <A. B. C. D>|all] IPv6 <X: X: : X: X>|all vrf (Optional) Include an IP address to clear neighbor entries for th at specific address, or use the all parameter to clear entries for all IP addresses. (Optional) Include an IPv6 address to clear neighbor entries for that specific address, or use the all parameter to clear entries fo r all IPv6 addresses. Clears IPv4 and IPv6 neighbor entries for the specified VRF or for all VRFs. If no VRF is specified he default VRF is cleared. Public clear arp 239 Clear neighbor entries for all VRFs Clear neighbor entries for the specified VRF. Syntax: clear arp.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the clear arp command?
Syntax: clear arp
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command do?

The debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command enables ARP security debug logs. If <SEVERITY> is omitted, all severities are logged. The no form of this command disables ARP security debug logs. <LOG-CATEGORY> severity <LEVEL> Selects the ARP security debug log category. Available categori es are: • all: Selects all ARP security debug log categories. • config: Selects the ARP security config debug log category. inspection: Selects the ARP security inspection debug log • category. • packet: Selects the ARP security packet debug log categor y. Specifies how to filter the ARP security debug logging by settin g the minimum severity level for which debug logging will be pe rformed. The selected severity level and all severities above (m ore severe) will be included in the logging. Syntax: debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command?
Syntax: debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 neighbor mac command do?

The IPv6 neighbor mac command specifies a permanent static neighbor entry in the ARP table (for IPv6 neighbors). The no form of this command deletes a permanent static neighbor entry from the ARP table. <IPv6-ADDR>> Specifies an IP address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. Range: 4096 to 131072. Default: 131072. Public IPv6 neighbor mac 245 mac <MAC-ADDR>> Specifies the MAC address of the neighbor (xx: xx: xx: xx: xx: xx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. Range: 4096 to 131072. Default: 131072. Syntax: IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the IPv6 neighbor mac command?
Syntax: IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show arp inspection interface [<IFNAME>] [vlan <VLAN-ID>] command do?

The show arp inspection interface [<IFNAME>] [vlan <VLAN-ID>] command shows the current configuration of dynamic ARP inspection on an interface. Specifies the interface. Specifies the VLAN ID. Range: 1 to 4094.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show arp inspection statistics vlan [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The show arp inspection statistics vlan [<VLAN-ID>] command shows statistics about forwarded and dropped ARP packets. When <VLAN-ID> is not specified, information is shown for all configured VLANs. Specifies the VLAN ID or range of IDs separated by a dash " - ". Range: 1 to 4094.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show arp inspection vlan [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The show arp inspection vlan [<VLAN-ID>] command shows the current configuration of dynamic ARP inspection on a VLAN. When <VLAN-ID> is not specified, information is shown for all configured VLANs. Specifies the VLAN ID or range of IDs separated by a dash " - ". Range: 1 to 4094.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show spanning-tree detail command do?
The show spanning-tree detail command shows spanning tree detail including CIST and corresponding port information. Syntax: show spanning-tree detail.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree detail command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree detail
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports command do?

The show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports command shows ports blocked by STP protection functions such as Root guard, Loop guard, BPDU guard, and RPVST guard in addition to MSTI information. Public show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports 208 Specifies the MSTP instance ID. Range: 1 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID>. Syntax: show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports [instance <INSTANCE-ID>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports [instance <INSTANCE-ID>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show spanning-tree mst command do?
The show spanning-tree mst command shows MSTP configuration and status information for each instance. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree mst command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree mst
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail 219 command do?

The show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail 219 command displays MSTP configurations for the given instance ID with corresponding port details. Specifies the MSTP instance number. Range: 0 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID>. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail 219 command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show spanning-tree mst interface command do?

The show spanning-tree mst interface command shows MSTP configurations for the given instance ID with corresponding port details. Specifies the MSTP instance number. Range: 0 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID> <IFNAME>. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> interface <IFNAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree mst interface command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> interface <IFNAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show spanning-tree summary port command do?
The show spanning-tree summary port command shows spanning tree port summary information. Syntax: show spanning-tree summary port.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree summary port command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree summary port
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show spanning-tree summary root command do?

The show spanning-tree summary root command shows spanning tree root summary information. Enables the spanning tree protocol on the switch. The no form of this command disables the spanning tree protocol on the switch. Syntax: show spanning-tree summary root.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree summary root command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree summary root
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the clear spanning-tree statistics (RPVST+) command?
Syntax: clear spanning-tree statistics [VLAN-ID]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show capacities rpvst command do?
The show capacities rpvst command shows the capacities of RPVST VLANs configurable on a system and RPVST VPORTs supported in a system. Syntax: show capacities rpvst.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show capacities rpvst command?
Syntax: show capacities rpvst
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show capacities-status rpvst command do?
The show capacities-status rpvst command shows the number of RPVST VLANs and RPVST VPORTs currently configured. Syntax: show capacities-status rpvst.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status rpvst command?
Syntax: show capacities-status rpvst
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show spanning-tree vlan command do?

The show spanning-tree vlan command displays the spanning tree mode and information on the RPVST instance of the specified VLAN. Specifies the number of a VLAN. <VLAN-ID>. Syntax: show spanning-tree vlan <VLAN-ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree vlan command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree vlan <VLAN-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show spanning-tree vlan detail command do?

The show spanning-tree vlan detail command displays the spanning tree mode and information on the RPVST instance of the specified VLAN and optionally displays details on the RPVST instance for the VLAN. Specifies the number of a VLAN. <VLAN-ID> Enables and configures the auto re-enable timeout in seconds for all interfaces with BPDU guard enabled. When an interface is disabled after receiving an unauthorized BPDU it will automatically be re-enabled after the timeout expires. The default is for the interface to stay disabled until manually re-enabled. The no form of the command disables BPDU guard timeout. This is the default. Specifies the re-enable timeout in seconds. Range: 1 to 6553 5. <INTERVAL> Configures use of extended system ID. When enabled, the VLAN ID is included in spanning tree packets. Syntax: show spanning-tree vlan <VLAN-ID> detail.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree vlan detail command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree vlan <VLAN-ID> detail
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the diag-dump private-vlan basic command?
Syntax: diag-dump private-vlan basic
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show capacities private-vlan command do?
The show capacities private-vlan command shows the maximum number of primary and secondary VLANs per domain and secondary ports per LC that can be configured. Syntax: show capacities private-vlan.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show capacities private-vlan command?
Syntax: show capacities private-vlan
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show capacities-status private-vlan command do?

The show capacities-status private-vlan command shows the number of primary VLANs currently configured and the maximum capacity of primary VLANs on the switch. Syntax: show capacities-status private-vlan.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status private-vlan command?
Syntax: show capacities-status private-vlan
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the interface lag command do?

The interface lag command creates a Link Aggregation Group (LAG) interface represented by an ID. The no form of this command deletes a LAG interface represented by an ID. Specifies a LAG interface ID. <ID>. Syntax: interface lag <ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the interface lag command?
Syntax: interface lag <ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the interface persona lag command do?

The interface persona lag command creates a persona LAG, a template for LAG (Link Aggregation Group) interfaces. A persona LAG defines configuration options that can be applied to multiple LAGs for consistency and simplified management. Only LAG interfaces can reference persona LAG templates. The persona LAG name cannot use reserved interface names or formats, such as vlan, lag, or loopback. Configurations defined in a persona LAG are valid only for LAG interfaces. Public interface persona lag 52 The no form of this command deletes the persona LAG interface. Specifies the name of the persona LAG. <NAME>. Syntax: interface persona lag <NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the interface persona lag command?
Syntax: interface persona lag <NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip address command do?

The ip address command sets an IPv4 address and subnet mask to a LAG interface. One primary and up to 31 secondary address can be configured per interface. The no form of this command removes the IPv4 address from the interface. Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255. You can remove leading zeros. For example, the address 192.169.005.100 becomes 192.168.5.1 00. Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 32. Specifies a secondary IP address. <IPv4-ADDR> <MASK> secondary. Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip address command?
Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show interface persona command do?
The show interface persona command shows all interface personas and their attached interfaces. Syntax: show interface persona.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show interface persona command?
Syntax: show interface persona
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show lacp interfaces command do?

The show lacp interfaces command displays an LACP configuration of the physical interfaces, including VSX s. If an interface name is passed as argument, it only displays an LACP configuration of a specified interface. Optional: Specifies an interface name. <IFNAME> vsx-peer Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Public show lacp interfaces 76. Syntax: show lacp interfaces [<IFNAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show lacp interfaces command?
Syntax: show lacp interfaces [<IFNAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show lag command?
Syntax: show lag <LAG-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show running-config interface command?
Syntax: show running-config interface
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vlan trunk native command do?

The vlan trunk native command assigns a native VLAN ID to a LAG interface. The no form of this command removes a native VLAN from a LAG interface and assigns VLAN ID 1 as its native VLAN. <VLAN-ID> Maximum number of VLANs supported: 512 (4100i) Specifies the number of the VLAN ID to assign. The VLAN ID m ust exist. Syntax: vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the vlan trunk native command?
Syntax: vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the diag-dump smartlink basic command do?

The diag-dump smartlink basic command dumps the Smartlink configuration, state and statistics. Configures primary port for a Smartlink group. The no form of this command removes primary port from a Smartlink group. Specifies interface for primary port. <INTERFACE-NAME> Configures secondary port for a Smartlink group. The no form of this command removes secondary port from a Smartlink group. Specifies interface for secondary port. <INTERFACE-NAME> Configures control VLAN in a Smartlink group. The no form of this command removes control VLAN from a Smartlink group. Specifies VLAN ID for a Smartlink group. Public control-vlan 102 Specifies VLANs protected by a Smartlink group. The no form of this command removes VLANs protected by a Smartlink group. Specifies list of VLAN IDs. Range is 1 to 4094. Syntax: diag-dump smartlink basic.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the diag-dump smartlink basic command?
Syntax: diag-dump smartlink basic
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show capacities smartlink command do?
The show capacities smartlink command shows Smartlink capacities or Smartlink capacities and status. Syntax: show capacities smartlink | show capacities-status smartlink.

syntax: show capacities smartlink | show capacities-status smartlink

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show capacities smartlink command?
Syntax: show capacities smartlink | show capacities-status smartlink

syntax: show capacities smartlink | show capacities-status smartlink

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show capacities ufd command do?

The show capacities ufd command command show capacities ufd shows UFD session capacity. Command show capacities-status ufd shows UFD session capacity and the number of UFD sessions configured. Syntax: show capacities ufd.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show capacities ufd command?
Syntax: show capacities ufd
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show running-config ufd command?
Syntax: show running-config ufd
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show-tech ufd command do?
The show-tech ufd command executes the show ufd command followed by the show running-config ufd command. Syntax: show-tech ufd.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show-tech ufd command?
Syntax: show-tech ufd
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what monitoring behavior is documented for Configuration version migration?

Configuration version migration: A configuration version migration occurred which re quired a reboot. Console error Fabric fault The console failed to start. A fabric fault occurred. All line modules faulted A zero line card condition occurred. Redundancy switchover requested A user requested a redundancy switchover.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what monitoring behavior is documented for Redundant Management election timeout?

Redundant Management election timeout: Critical service fault (error) A failure to elect a standby management module in t he allotted time. A daemon critical to switch operation has stopped fu nctioning. An extra error string may be present to d escribe the error in detail. VSF autojoin renumber Reset triggered by VSF autojoin. VSF member renumbered A user requested a renumber of a VSF member. VSF switchover requested A user requested a VSF switchover.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the VSX software update command do?
The VSX software update command chassis unsupported PSUs/fans Unsupported or misconfigured PSUs or system fans.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what monitoring behavior is documented for State information: Disabled by VSX?

State information: Disabled by VSX: Link state: down for 3 days (since Tue Mar 16 05: 20: 47 UTC 2021) Link transitions: 0 Description: Hardware: Ethernet, MAC address: 04: 09: 73: 62: 90: e7 MTU 1500 Type SFP+DAC3 Full-duplex qos trust none Speed 0 Mb/s Auto-negotiation is off Flow-control: off Error-control: off.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear mirror command do?

The clear mirror command clears the mirror statistics for all configured mirror sessions or a specified session Specifies all configured sessions. Specifies a numeric identifier for the session. Range: 1 to 4. Syntax: clear mirror [all | <SESSION-ID>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the clear mirror command?
Syntax: clear mirror [all | <SESSION-ID>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the comment <COMMENT> command do?

The comment <COMMENT> command specifies a comment for the mirroring session. When used in mirror endpoint command context, specifies a comment for the mirror endpoint. The no form of this command removes the comment. A comment string of up to 64 characters composed of letters, n umbers, underscores, dashes, spaces, and periods. <COMMENT>. Syntax: comment <COMMENT>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the comment <COMMENT> command?
Syntax: comment <COMMENT>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the destination interface {<INTERFACE-ID>|<LAG-NAME>} command?
Syntax: destination interface {<INTERFACE-ID>|<LAG-NAME>}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the disable command do?
The disable command disables the mirroring session specified by the current command context. Syntax: disable.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the disable command?
Syntax: disable
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the enable command do?
The enable command enables the mirroring session for the current command context. Syntax: enable.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the enable command?
Syntax: enable
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the mirror session <SESSION-ID> command do?

The mirror session <SESSION-ID> command creates a mirroring session configuration context or enters an existing mirroring session configuration context. From this context, you can enter commands to configure and enable or disable the mirroring session. The no form of this command removes an existing mirroring session from the configuration. Specifies the session identifier. Range: 1 to 4 <SESSION-ID>. Syntax: mirror session <SESSION-ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the mirror session <SESSION-ID> command?
Syntax: mirror session <SESSION-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the lldp med poe [priority-override] command do?

The lldp med poe [priority-override] command enables MED TLV list in LLDP to advertise for Power over Ethernet Data Link Layer Classification. Also enables the lldp-MED TLV priority to override user configured port priority for Power over Ethernet. When both dot3 and MED are enabled, dot 3 will take precedence. MED TLV is by default enabled for PoE. Priority over-ride is by default disabled. The no form of this command disables MED TLV list in LLDP. [priority-override] System defined name of the interface. Syntax: lldp med poe [priority-override].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the lldp med poe [priority-override] command?
Syntax: lldp med poe [priority-override]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the power-over-ethernet command do?

The power-over-ethernet command enables per-interface power distribution. Per-port power is enabled by default with priority low. PoE cannot be disabled for individual ports when Quick PoE is enabled for the entire switch or line module. The no form of this command disables per-interface power distribution. Syntax: power-over-ethernet.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the power-over-ethernet command?
Syntax: power-over-ethernet
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the power-over-ethernet allocate-by {usage | class} command do?

The power-over-ethernet allocate-by {usage | class} command configures the power allocation method. Power allocation method is initially based on usage. PSE Allocated power value will change to LLDP negotiated power if and when LLDP exchange takes place between PSE and PD. When there is no LLDP negotiation, PSE Allocated Power Value will be the actual instantaneous power draw and reserve power based on actual consumption. In allocate-by class, power allocation is based on PD requested class and PSE allocated power value will be the LLDP negotiated power when LLDP exchange takes place between PSE and PD. When there is no LLDP negotiation, PSE Allocate Power will be based on PD class. Reserve power is based on PD Class. By default, power allocation is by usage. Syntax: power-over-ethernet allocate-by {usage | class}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the power-over-ethernet allocate-by {usage | class} command?
Syntax: power-over-ethernet allocate-by {usage | class}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the power-over-ethernet pre-std-detect command do?

The power-over-ethernet pre-std-detect command before IEEE 802.3 released the first Power over Ethernet standard (802.3af), vendors had shipped PoE capable switches and PD's. HPE Aruba Networking switches are backward compatible and will support both IEEE standard and pre-standard 802.3af Power over Ethernet PD's concurrently. This CLI allows the user to enable or disable pre-802.3af-standard device detection and powering on the specific port. When pre-std-detect is enabled, power will be delivered on PairA only. Default is disabled. The no form of this command resets the action to default. Syntax: power-over-ethernet pre-std-detect.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the power-over-ethernet pre-std-detect command?
Syntax: power-over-ethernet pre-std-detect
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low} command do?

The power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low} command sets PoE priority for an interface Specifying critical, high, or low indicates the priority of the interface in the event of power over-subscription. Within the same priority level, higher power-priority line-module ports have higher precedence. With same PoE priority and same line-module priority, lower numbered line-module ports have higher precedence. Per-interface PoE priority is low by default. The no form of this command resets the priority to default PoE priority "low". Syntax: power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low} command?
Syntax: power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the power-over-ethernet threshold <PERCENTAGE> command do?

The power-over-ethernet threshold <PERCENTAGE> command sets the threshold at which the system will send an excess power consumption notification trap. Default value is 80 percentage. The no form of this command resets the action to default. Excess power consumption trap threshold. Range 1-99. <PERCENTAGE>. Syntax: power-over-ethernet threshold <PERCENTAGE>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the power-over-ethernet threshold <PERCENTAGE> command?
Syntax: power-over-ethernet threshold <PERCENTAGE>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the power-over-ethernet trap command do?

The power-over-ethernet trap command this command enables/disables the SNMP trap generation for PoE related events at system level. PoE trap generation is enabled by default. The no form of this command resets the priority to default PoE priority "low". Syntax: power-over-ethernet trap.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the power-over-ethernet trap command?
Syntax: power-over-ethernet trap
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what monitoring behavior is documented for Interface and VLAN management?

Interface and VLAN management: Initiates an SSH connection from Aruba AirWave to AOS-CX so that the device outputs from the AOS-CX CLI can be displayed in the Aruba AirWave user inter face. Firmware versions Displays neighbor devices connected to AOS-CX switches Device topology Public Supported features with AirWave and the AOS-CX swi... 102 S u p p o r t e d f e a t u r e s w i t h A i r W a v e a n d t h e A O S - C X s w i...

section: Configuring the AOS-CX switch to be monitored by AirWave

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, how do you configure the AOS-CX switch to be monitored by AirWave?

To configure the AOS-CX switch to be monitored by AirWave, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable SNMP on the switch by entering the snmp-server vrf command 2. Configure the SNMP v2C community to public by entering the snmp-server community publ 3. The community-string is used by SNMP v1 and SNMP v2C for unencrypted authentication. SNMP v3 lets.

mvrp Sends Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP): notifications. ospf Sends Open Shortest Path First (OSPFv2) notifications. ospfv3 Sends Open Shortest Path First version 3 (OSPFv3) notifications. port-security Sends Port Security notifications. power Sends Power notifications. power-ethernet Sends Power over Ethernet (PoE) notifications. rmon Sends Remote Network Monitoring (RMON) notifications. rpvst Sends Rapid Per VLANspanning Tree (RPVST) notifications.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmpv3 user <USER-NAME> view <VIEW-NAME> command do?

The snmpv3 user <USER-NAME> view <VIEW-NAME> command associates a user with an existing SNMP MIB view. The no form of this command removes the associated user from the specified SNMP MIB view. Specifies the user name for the SNMP MIB view. Accepts a maxi mum of 32 characters. Public snmpv3 user view 122 Specifies the view name for the SNMP MIB view. Accepts a maxi mum of 32 characters. Syntax: snmpv3 user <USER-NAME> view <VIEW-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the snmpv3 user <USER-NAME> view <VIEW-NAME> command?
Syntax: snmpv3 user <USER-NAME> view <VIEW-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP)?

Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP): In a network where IP multicast traffic is transmitted for various multimedia applications, you can use the switch to reduce unnecessary bandwidth usage on a per-port basis by configuring IGMP (Internet Group Management Protocol). IGMPv3 (RFC 3376) and IGMPv2 (RFC 2236) are the current RFCs for IGMP. In the factory default state (IGMP disabled), the switch simply floods all IP multicast traffic it receives on a given VLAN through all ports on that VLAN (except the port on which it received the traffic). This can result in significant and unnecessary bandwidth usage in networks where IP multicast traffic is a factor. Enabling IGMP allows the ports to detect IGMP queries and report packets and manage IP multicast traffic through the switch. IGMP is useful in multimedia applications such as LAN TV, desktop conferencing, and collaborative computing, where there is Multi Point communication; that is, communication from one to many hosts, or communication originating from many hosts and destined for many other hosts. Public.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp apply access-list command do?

The ip igmp apply access-list command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. The no form of this command unconfigures the rules set for the ACL. NOTE This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Associates an ACL with the IGMP. Specifies the name of the ACL. Syntax: ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip igmp apply access-list command?
Syntax: ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp last-member-query-interval command do?

The ip igmp last-member-query-interval command configures an IGMP last member query interval value in seconds on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the value to a default of 1 second on an interface. Specifies an IGMP last-member-query-interval on the inter face. Default: 1 second. Range: 1-2 seconds. <INTERVAL-VALUE> Public ip igmp last-member-query-interval 29. Syntax: ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip igmp last-member-query-interval command?
Syntax: ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp querier interval command do?

The ip igmp querier interval command configures the interval between IGMP queries on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the IGMP querier interval to the default value of 125 seconds on an interface. Specifies the IGMP querier interval in seconds on the interface. Default: 125 seconds. Range: 5-300. <INTERVAL-VALUE>. Syntax: ip igmp querier interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip igmp querier interval command?
Syntax: ip igmp querier interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp querier-wait-time command do?

The ip igmp querier-wait-time command configures initial IGMP querier-wait-time value in seconds. The no form of this command sets the IGMP querier-wait-time to the default value of 260 seconds. Note that the wait timer can be configured to any numbers within the 1-300 second range. Configures IGMP querier-wait-time to desired value. <QUERIER-WAIT-TIME>. Syntax: ip igmp querier-wait-time <QUERIER-WAIT-TIME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip igmp querier-wait-time command?
Syntax: ip igmp querier-wait-time <QUERIER-WAIT-TIME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp querier query-max-response-time command do?

The ip igmp querier query-max-response-time command configures the IGMP querier max response time value in seconds on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the querier max response time value to the default of 10 seconds on an interface. Specifies the IGMP querier max response time value on the inte rface. Default: 10 seconds. Range: 10-128 seconds. <RESPONSE-TIME> Public ip igmp querier query-max-response-time 34. Syntax: ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip igmp querier query-max-response-time command?
Syntax: ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp robustness command do?

The ip igmp robustness command configures IGMP robustness on an interface, depending on the command context. The robustness parameter allows tuning for the expected packet loss on a subnet. The no form of this command sets the robustness value to the default of 2 on an interface. Public ip igmp robustness 35 Specifies an IGMP robustness value on the interface. Default: 2. Range: 1-7. <VALUE>. Syntax: ip igmp robustness <VALUE>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip igmp robustness command?
Syntax: ip igmp robustness <VALUE>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp router-alert-check command do?

The ip igmp router-alert-check command enables or disables IGMP router alert check for IGMP packets. IGMP packets without the router alert field set are dropped if router alert check is enabled. Router alert check is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables router alert check for IGMP packets. Enable IGMP router alert check. Disable IGMP router alert check. Enabling IGMP router alert check on interface VLAN 2: switch(config)# interface vlan 2 switch(config-if-vlan)# ip igmp router-alert-check enable Disabling IGMP router alert check on interface VLAN 2: switch(config)# interface vlan 2 switch(config-if-vlan)# ip igmp router-alert-check disable switch(config)# interface vlan 2 switch(config-if-vlan)# no ip igmp router-alert-check enable. Syntax: ip igmp router-alert-check [enable | disable].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip igmp router-alert-check command?
Syntax: ip igmp router-alert-check [enable | disable]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip igmp version strict command do?

The ip igmp version strict command configures an IGMP strict version on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. Drops packets that do not match the configured version. The no form of the command removes the strict version configuration from the interface. Specifies the IGMP version on the interface. Select 2 for IGMPv 2 (RFC2236). Select 3 for IGMPv3 (RFC3376). Values: 2 or 3. <VERSION> Public ip igmp version strict 40. Syntax: ip igmp version <VERSION> strict.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the ip igmp version strict command?
Syntax: ip igmp version <VERSION> strict
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show ip igmp counters command do?
The show ip igmp counters command shows IGMP counter details, or shows counters by VRF. Specify all-vrfs to show information for all VRFs. all-vrfs. Syntax: show ip igmp counters [all-vrfs].

Syntax: show ip igmp counters [all-vrfs]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show ip igmp group command do?

The show ip igmp group command shows IGMP joined group information for the specified group, or shows joined group source and display information by VRF. Specifies the IP address of the group. Format: A. B. C. D <GROUP-IP> source <SOURCE-IP> Specifies the IP address of the source. Format: A. B. C. D Specify all-vrfs to show information for all VRFs. all-vrfs. Syntax: show ip igmp group <GROUP-IP> [source <SOURCE-IP>] [all-vrfs].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp group command?
Syntax: show ip igmp group <GROUP-IP> [source <SOURCE-IP>] [all-vrfs]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show ip igmp groups command do?

The show ip igmp groups command shows IGMP group information, or you can display group information by VRF. Specify all-vrfs to show information for all VRFs. all-vrfs. Syntax: show ip igmp groups [all-vrfs].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp groups command?
Syntax: show ip igmp groups [all-vrfs]

The no form of this command disables the rules set for the ACL. NOTE This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Associates an ACL with the IGMP. Specifies the name of the ACL. <ACL-NAME> NOTE If the access list is configured for both L2 VLA N and L3 VLAN, the L3 VLAN configuration will be applied. The IPv6 mld snooping fastlearn command this command enables the port to learn group information on receiving topology change notification. For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the IPv6 mld snooping report-suppression command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping report-suppression command enable MLD report suppression to minimize unnecessary multicast control traffic by aggregating and suppressing duplicate reports for the same group from multiple clients within a VLAN. MLD Report Suppression only works with MLDv1 reports, not MLDv2. However, if MLDv2 is explicitly configured, this feature still will suppress reports for MLDv1 join and leave messages. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping report-suppression enable|disable.

The IPv6 mld apply access-list command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the MLD join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. The IPv6 mld querier interval command this command configures MLD querier interval. The default interval-value is 125. Required: 5-300, configures MLD querier interval. NOTE Default interval-value is 125. Use the no ipv 6 mld querier interval command to set interval -value to the default. Public IPv6 mld querier interval 124. messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, how do you configure QoS?

To configure QoS, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure how values are assigned to ingress packets with the commands qos dscp-map, and qo 2. Optionally, add a rate limit for ingress traffic on one or more interfaces with the command rate-lim 3. If you do not want to use the default QoS queue profile for queue mapping, create one or more custom 4. If you do not want to use the default QoS schedule profile to determine the order in which queues are. Commands shown in the procedure include: QoS configuration 20.

document_title: Quality Of Service Guide (4100I, 6000, 6100 Switch Series)

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the apply qos threshold-profile command do?

The apply qos threshold-profile command applies a threshold profile globally to all Ethernet and LAG interfaces on the switch, or to a specific interface. When applied globally, the specified threshold profile is configured only on Ethernet interfaces and LAGs that do not already have their own schedule profile. The same profile can be applied both globally and locally to an interface. This guarantees that an interface always uses the specified threshold profile, even if the global profile is changed. The no form of this command removes the specified threshold profile from an interface, and causes it to use the global threshold profile. This is the only way to remove a threshold profile override from an interface. A profile can only be deleted once it is no longer applied to any interface. Syntax: apply qos threshold-profile <THRESHOLD-NAME>.

document_title: Quality Of Service Guide (4100I, 6000, 6100 Switch Series)

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the apply qos threshold-profile command?
Syntax: apply qos threshold-profile <THRESHOLD-NAME>

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos egress-rewrite command do?

The qos egress-rewrite command enables a rewrite of the 802.1Q VLAN priority code point (PCP) or DSCP values for all traffic egressing on the configured interface. The PCP or DHCP value is determined by a globally configured egress priority rewrite map based on the traffic's local priority. Use this command to configure a system-wide mapping from internal local priorities to 802.1Q VLAN priority code point (PCP) or DSCP values. Public qos egress-rewrite 46 The no form of this command disables egress priority rewrite on the interface. Use show qos egress-rewrite-map to view the current global egress priority rewrite map. Use show qos egress-rewrite-map default to view the factory default egress priority rewrite map. Syntax: qos egress-rewrite [cos|dscp].

document_title: Quality Of Service Guide (4100I, 6000, 6100 Switch Series)

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the qos egress-rewrite command?
Syntax: qos egress-rewrite [cos|dscp]

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos queue-profile command do?

The qos queue-profile command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile.. A queue profile maps queues to CoS values. Each profile has two, four, or eight queues numbered 0 to 7. The larger the queue number, the higher its priority during transmission scheduling. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS queue profile. Only profiles that are not currently applied can be removed. <NAME> Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). Public qos queue-profile 50. Syntax: qos queue-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos schedule-profile command do?

The qos schedule-profile command creates a QoS schedule profile and switches to the config-schedule context for the profile. If the specified schedule profile exists, this command switches to the config-schedule context for the profile. The schedule profile determines the order in which queues are selected to transmit a packet, and the amount of service defined for each queue. Public qos schedule-profile 51 Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). <NAME>. Syntax: qos schedule-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos threshold-profile command do?

The qos threshold-profile command creates a QoS threshold profile and switches to the config-threshold context for the profile. If the specified threshold profile exists, this command switches to the config-threshold context for the existing profile. The threshold profile determines the action to take when a threshold is exceeded for each queue. A threshold profile is composed of up to. Each queue defines the action to take when buffer utilization exceeds a specific threshold. Configure queues with the command queue. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS threshold profile. Only profiles that are not currently applied to an interface can be removed. NOTE In 4100i Switch Series, only one profile can be created. Specifies the name of the QoS threshold profile to create or co nfigure. Syntax: qos threshold-profile <NAME>.

document_title: Quality Of Service Guide (4100I, 6000, 6100 Switch Series)

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the WRED command do?

The WRED command defines the threshold settings and action for a specified queue in a threshold-profile. For ECN, when queue utilization exceeds the threshold value, ECT (ECN-Capable Transport) packets will be CE (Congestion Encountered) marked when transmitted. For WRED, when queue utilization exceeds the threshold value, WRED action will randomly early-drop packets to signal congestion. More than one WRED action can be configured on a single queue for different packet colors. The no form of this command removes the settings for a queue. NOTE • Threshold-profile actions are only supported for traffic arriving on interfaces configured in the trust dscp mode. • ECN is not supported in 4100i Switch Series. Specifies the queue number. Range: 0 to 7. <0-7> <WRED-MIN-LIMIT> <WRED-MAX-LIMIT>. Syntax: WRED.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the https-server authentication password command do?

The https-server authentication password command enables authentication using username and password, which corresponds to the default authentication mechanism. Enabling the password authentication mode disables the certificate authentication mode. NOTE Only one authentication method can be enabled at a time. Syntax: https-server authentication password.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the https-server max-user-sessions command do?

The https-server max-user-sessions command sets the maximum amount of concurrent open sessions for any given user through the HTTPS server. The amount of concurrent open sessions may have an impact on system performance, so it is recommended to set this value to the minimum necessary. Specifies the maximum number of user sessions allowed. Defaul t: 6. Maximum value: 8. <SESSION-AMT>. Syntax: https-server max-user-sessions <SESSION-AMT>.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the https-server rest access-mode command do?

The https-server rest access-mode command changes the REST API access mode. The default mode is read-write. This command does not affect Central connections, which have permission to alter configurations regardless of the access mode set on the switch. Selects the read/write mode. Allows POST, PUT, PATCH, and DE LETE methods to be called on all configurable elements in the s witch database. Selects the read-only mode. Write access to most switch resou rces through the REST API is disabled. Setting the mode to read-write on the REST API allows POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE methods to be called on all configurable elements in the switch database. By default, REST APIs in the device are in the read-write mode. Some switch resources allow POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE regardless of REST API mode. Syntax: https-server rest access-mode {read-only | read-write}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the https-server rest access-mode command?
Syntax: https-server rest access-mode {read-only | read-write}

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about VLAN interfaces?

VLAN interfaces: Hardware (system) interfaces • Hardware interfaces are of type system. • Hardware interfaces are included in the database automatically. • Interfaces of type system cannot be added or deleted. LAG interfaces • LAG interfaces are of type lag. • You can use the DELETE method to delete a LAG interface. Example of creating a LAG interface with member ports 1/1/1 and 1/1/2: Method and URI: POST "/rest/v10.xx/system/interfaces" Request body: Public Considerations for ports and interfaces 60 { "name": "lag50", "vrf": "/rest/v10.xx/system/vrfs/default", "type": "lag", "interfaces": [ "/rest/v10.xx/system/interfaces/1%2F1%2F1", "/rest/v10.xx/system/interfaces/1%2F1%2F2" ] }.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about Valid VLAN collection attribute list?

Valid VLAN collection attribute list: GET https://<IP>/rest/<version>/system/vlans/1? attributes=name, type, admin { "admin": "up", "name": "DEFAULT_VLAN_1", "type": "default" } Public Attributes parameter 64 Count parameter Syntax The count parameter indicates the request wants to obtain the total number of entries related to a resource. A successful response contains a JSON format with the count key and a number as the value. It works with requests including wildcard and other query parameters. Omitting it in the request is equivalent to specifying count=false. Including it in the request as count= is equivalent to specifying count=true. A request with count to a specific resource is valid and will return 1. Use Cases The user gets the number of entries instead of the values of entries. Requests to dynamic or protected resources are not limited. Limitations It is not supported for notifications. Examples Single resource A request with a count to a specific resource is valid and will return 1.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about CLI Management Interface?

CLI Management Interface: Introduction Firmware Site Distribution (FWSD) is an efficient feature designed to allow the client to upgrade multiple devices in a network simultaneously. The orchestrator coordinates the entire upgrade process, providing better control and monitoring. The feature can handle a large number of devices, making it suitable for networks of various sizes. Devices in the network can retrieve firmware images from seed devices, minimizing the load on external sources. This feature can be used by a CLI command or a REST request. It's possible to execute the firmware site distribution via CLI and the REST management interface request at the same time. The switch can handle up to 2 concurrent requests if the requests share the same SSL session. Although it is possible, it is recommended to use only one to avoid any inconvenience. FWSD is particularly suitable for environments with a large number of devices which are locally co-located. FWSD operates as follows: 1. The orchestrator determines which devices require upgrades. 2. The orchestrator sends upgrade commands to the pending devices. 3.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth command do?
The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth command enables or disables MAC authentication globally or at the port-level. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth {enable | disable}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth {enable | disable}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command configures the RADIUS authentication method for MAC authentication. Following are the MAC authentication methods supported: • CHAP Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-metho... 628 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h a u t h - m e t h o... • PAP NOTE The PEAP-MSCHAPv2 method of authentication is not supported. The no form of the command resets the authentication method to the default, chap. Enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows MAC reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users who are already authenticated, retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables cached reauthentication. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}.

syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command?

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the cached reauthentication period (in seconds). Defa ult: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command configures the period during which the switch does not try to authenticate a rejected client. The no form of the command resets the quiet period to the default, 60 seconds. Specifies the quiet period (in seconds). Default: 60. Range: 0 to 65535. <PERIOD>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-period <PERIOD>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-period <PERIOD>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command configures the MAC authentication server group globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the authentication server group to the default value, radius. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius ser... 634 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h r a d i u s s e r... When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>.

syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command enables periodic MAC reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic MAC reauthentication on the port. Configures the period after which MAC authenticated clients must be reauthenticated on the port. You must first enable MAC reauthentication on the port before configuring the MAC reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the MAC reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the MAC reauthentication period (in seconds). Default: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth.

Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access client-limit m command do?

The aaa authentication port-access client-limit m command configures the data client limit on the multidomain enabled interface. By default, the data client limit on a multidomain enabled interface is 1, and the maximum number of data clients supported on a multidomain enabled port is 5. Specifies the maximum data client limit on the multidomain ena bled interface. Range: 1 to 5. <DATA-CLIENT-LIMIT>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access client-limit multi-domain <DATA-CLIENT-LIMIT>.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear dot1x supplicant statistics command do?

The clear dot1x supplicant statistics command clears the 802.1X supplicant statistics associated with the interface. If no interface is specified, the statistics are cleared for all 802.1X supplicant-enabled interfaces. Specifies the range of VLAN interfaces for which the supplicant statistics are cleared. <IFRANGE> Configures the time period (in seconds) to wait for a potential 802.1X authenticator on the other end before considering the link to be non-802.1X-capable and opening the interface on the data-plane. On a timeout, the switch will not use the authentication result to determine the forwarding behavior of the interface until a link flap. If not set, the switch will wait for the 802.1X authentication cycle to complete before determining the forwarding state of the interface. Syntax: clear dot1x supplicant statistics [interface <IFRANGE>].

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show aaa authentication port-access dot1x sup command do?

The show aaa authentication port-access dot1x sup command shows the 802.1X supplicant statistics on each 802.1X supplicant-enabled interface. Specifies the range of VLAN interfaces for which the supplicant status is shown. Specifies a VLAN interface for which the supplicant status is sh own. <IFRANGE> vlan <VLAN-ID> Shows the 802.1X supplicant status on each 802.1X supplicant-enabled interface. <IFRANGE> vlan <VLAN-ID> Specifies the range of VLAN interfaces for which the supplicant status is shown. Specifies a VLAN interface for which the supplicant status is sh own. Configures the forwarding behavior of the interface on the data-plane when the authentication is in- progress during the first run of the supplicant. Default: start-open. The no form of the command resets it to the default. Syntax: show aaa authentication port-access dot1x supplicant statistics.
