messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa radius-attribute group command do?

The aaa radius-attribute group command configures an existing RADIUS server group for which the configured RADIUS attributes will be included in request packets. Enters the config-radius-attr context. The no form of this command unconfigures the RADIUS server group for the configured RADIUS attributes. NOTE Nas-id and tunnel-private-group-id attributes only apply to port access requests. Nas-ip-addr attributes only apply to management user requests. Specifies an existing RADIUS server group name. <GROUP-NAME> For the selected (by context) RADIUS server group, configures the Network Access Server (NAS) ID request type for which the attribute configured with command nas-id value will be included. Public nas-id request-type 835 The no form of this command unconfigures the specified request type. Syntax: aaa radius-attribute group <GROUP-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the aaa radius-attribute group command?
Syntax: aaa radius-attribute group <GROUP-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show port-access port-security interface port command do?

The show port-access port-security interface port command shows port security statistics for the ports in a switch. The output can be filtered by interface. Public show port-access port-security interface port-stat... 863 s h o w p o r t - a c c e s s p o r t - s e c u r i t y i n t e r f a c e p o r t - s t a t... Selects all interfaces. Specifies the interface name. Shows information about the sticky-mac client move violation. The output can be filtered by interface. Selects all interfaces. Specifies the interface name. Enables sticky learning on the port. All the existing and new MAC s learned on the port are made sticky. The no form of this command disables the sticky learning on the port. Syntax: show port-access port-security interface {all|<IF-NAME>} port-statistics.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show port-access port-security interface port command?
Syntax: show port-access port-security interface {all|<IF-NAME>} port-statistics
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the class (port-access gbp) command?
Syntax: [<SEQUENCE-NUMBER>] class {gbp-ip | gbp-IPv6 | gbp-mac} <CLASS-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about VLAN Add or Delete?

VLAN Add or Delete: The index for the ieee8021QBridge VLANstatic Table and dot1q VLANstatic Table is VLAN. To create or delete a VLAN, either configure: • ieee8021QBridge VLANstatic Row Status Mib object in ieee8021QBridge VLANstatic Table or • dot1QBridge VLANstatic Row Status Mib object in dot1q VLANstatic Table Set the value to 4 to create a new VLAN, and set the value to 6 to delete an existing VLAN.

The snmp-server view new_view aruba Wired Module Name included command sys Up Time Instance 1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0 snmp TrapOID 1.3.6.1.6.3.1.1.4.1 event Index 1.3.6.1.2.1.16.9.1.1.1 Contains the uptime for the syste m in centiseconds Contains the OID for the event log trap Contains an index that uniquely id entifies an event event Description 1.3.6.1.2.1.16.9.1.1.2 Contains the event log message Link-up and link-down traps Standard IF-MIB link-up and link-down traps will be sent on link-state change when an interface is configured using trap link-status or when user_config: link_state_snmp_trap is set to true. messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, how do you configure SNMP?

To configure SNMP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Set the system contact, location, and description for the switch with the following commands: 2. Configure the trap receivers to which the SNMP agent will send trap notifications with the command 3. Create an SNMP v3 context and associate it with any available SNMP v3 user to perform context specific 4. Create an SNMP v3 context and associate it with an available SNMP v1/v2c community string to perform 5. Review your SNMP configuration settings with the following commands: . Commands shown in the procedure include: snmp-server agent-port.; snmp-server host..
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index <INDEX> | all} command do?

The rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index <INDEX> | all} command enables and disables the RMON alarm and its index. RMON alarm is enabled by default. Enables the RMON alarm index Disables the RMON alarm index. Public rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index | all} 31 Specifies the RMON alarm index. Range: 1 to 20. all. Syntax: rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index <INDEX> | all}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index <INDEX> | all} command?
Syntax: rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index <INDEX> | all}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show mac-notify port command do?

The show mac-notify port command displays the MAC notification configuration on a range of ports. [port <PORTS>] Specifies a port, range of ports, or list of ports. Syntax: show mac-notify [port <PORTS>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show mac-notify port command?
Syntax: show mac-notify [port <PORTS>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show snmp agent-port command do?
The show snmp agent-port command displays SNMP agent UDP port number. Syntax: show snmp agent-port.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show snmp agent-port command?
Syntax: show snmp agent-port
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show snmp community command do?
The show snmp community command displays a list of all configured SNMP v1/v2c communities. Syntax: show snmp community.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show snmp community command?
Syntax: show snmp community
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show snmpv3 context command do?
The show snmpv3 context command displays all configured SNMP contexts. Syntax: show snmpv3 context.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 context command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 context
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show snmpv3 engine-id command do?

The show snmpv3 engine-id command displays the configured SNMP v3 snmp engine-id. If the SNMP v3 engine-id is not configured, by default a unique engine-id is created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Syntax: show snmpv3 engine-id.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 engine-id command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 engine-id
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show snmpv3 security-level command do?
The show snmpv3 security-level command displays the configured SNMP v3 security level. Syntax: show snmpv3 security-level.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 security-level command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 security-level
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show snmp system command do?
The show snmp system command displays SNMP description, location, and contact information. Syntax: show snmp system.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show snmp system command?
Syntax: show snmp system
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show snmp trap command do?
The show snmp trap command displays all configured SNMP traps/informs receivers. Syntax: show snmp trap.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show snmp trap command?
Syntax: show snmp trap
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show snmpv3 users command do?
The show snmpv3 users command displays all configured SNMP v3 users. For more details on the user enabled status, see snmpv3 security-level. Syntax: show snmpv3 users.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 users command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 users
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show snmp views command do?

The show snmp views command displays the list of all the configured SNMP views. pending_validation Default value that indicates SNMP view is yet to be validated. operational OID and mask validated. Invalid OID/mask. Validation failed for reasons other than OID/mask. invalid failed. Syntax: show snmp views.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show snmp views command?
Syntax: show snmp views
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show snmp vrf command do?
The show snmp vrf command displays the VRF on which the SNMP agent service is running. Public show snmp vrf 48. Syntax: show snmp vrf.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the show snmp vrf command?
Syntax: show snmp vrf
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command do?

The snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command creates an SNMP v3 context on the specified VRF. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP context. Public SNMP commands 49 Specifies the name of the context. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASC II characters, excluding space and question mark (?). <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the VRF associated with the context. Default: default. community <STRING> Specifies the SNMP community string associated with the con text. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question mark. Default: public. Syntax: snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command?
Syntax: snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command do?

The snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command configures the SNMP v3 SNMP engine-id allowing an administrator to configure a unique SNMP engine-id for the switch. This engine-id is used by the NMS management tool to identify and distinguish multiple switches on the same network. The no form of this command restores the default engine-id, created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. SNMP v3 SNMP engine-id in colon separated hexadecimal n otation. <ENGINE-ID>. Syntax: snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command?
Syntax: snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server agent-port command do?

The snmp-server agent-port command sets the UDP port number that the SNMP master agent uses to communicate. UDP port 161 is the default port. The no form of this command sets the SNMP master agent port to the default value. Specifies the UDP port number that the SNMP master agent wil l use. Range: 1 to 65535. Default: 161. <PORT> Public snmp-server agent-port 53. Syntax: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the snmp-server agent-port command?
Syntax: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>

section: mvrp Sends Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP)

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the guide say about mvrp Sends Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP)?

mvrp Sends Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP): notifications. ospf Sends Open Shortest Path First (OSPFv2) notifications. ospfv3 Sends Open Shortest Path First version 3 (OSPFv3) notifications. port-security Sends Port Security notifications. power Sends Power notifications. power-ethernet Sends Power over Ethernet (PoE) notifications. rmon Sends Remote Network Monitoring (RMON) notifications. rpvst Sends Rapid Per VLANspanning Tree (RPVST) notifications.

topic: mvrp Sends Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP)

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server system-contact command do?

The snmp-server system-contact command sets SNMP contact information. The no form of this command removes the SNMP contact information. Specifies SNMP contact information. Range: 1 to 128 printable ASCII characters, except for question mark (?). <INFO>. Syntax: snmp-server system-contact <INFO>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-contact command?
Syntax: snmp-server system-contact <INFO>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server system-location command do?

The snmp-server system-location command sets the SNMP location information. The no form of this command removes the SNMP location information. Specifies the SNMP location information. Range: 1 to 128 prin table ASCII characters, except for the question mark (?). Public snmp-server system-location 67. Syntax: snmp-server system-location <INFO>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-location command?
Syntax: snmp-server system-location <INFO>

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what SNMP MIB information is documented for 10.0.0.1?
The SNMP MIB reference documents 10.0.0.1. OID: 10.0.0.1. Description: switch(config)# snmp-server trap-source 10.0.0.1 vrf red.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server vrf command do?

The snmp-server vrf command configures a VRF on which the SNMP agent listens for incoming requests. By default, the SNMP agent does not listen on any VRF. 4100i, 6000, and 6100 only support default VRF. The SNMP agent can listen on multiple VRFs. The no form of this command stops the SNMP agent from listening for incoming requests on the specified VRF. Specifies the name of a VRF. <VRF-NAME>. Syntax: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the snmp-server vrf command?
Syntax: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the snmp-server trap module command?
Syntax: snmp-server trap module
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server trap port-security command do?

The snmp-server trap port-security command enables SNMP port-security violation traps on the system. Port-security violation traps are enabled by default. The no form of this command disables the SNMP port-security violation traps on the system. Specifies SNMP traps for port-security. Public snmp-server trap port-security 83. Syntax: snmp-server trap port-security.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the snmp-server trap port-security command?
Syntax: snmp-server trap port-security
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what SNMP MIB information is documented for v2-MIB?
The SNMP MIB reference documents v2-MIB. Description: SNMP v2-MIB.mib.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.18, what SNMP MIB information is documented for POWER-ETHERNET-MIB?
The SNMP MIB reference documents POWER-ETHERNET-MIB. Description: POWER-ETHERNET-MIB.
